author: Jason Mraz

text: I do not believe in taking the right decision, I take a decision and make it right.
author: Muhammad Ali Jinnah

text: You must give everything to make your life as beautiful as the dreams that dance in your imagination.
author: Roman Payne

text: Transformation is my favorite game and in my experience, anger and frustration are the result of you not being authentic somewhere in your life or with someone in your life. Being fake about anything creates a block inside of you. Life can’t work for you if you don’t show up as you.
author: Jason Mraz

text: I am always sad, I think. Perhaps this signifies that I am not sad at all, because sadness is something lower than your normal disposition, and I am always the same thing. Perhaps I am the only person in the world, then, who never becomes sad. Perhaps I am lucky.
author: Jonathan Safran Foer, Everything Is Illuminated

text: To look life in the face, always, to look life in the face, and to know it for what it is...at last, to love it for what it is, and then, to put it away...
author: Virginia Woolf

text: Every book has a soul, the soul of the person who wrote it and the soul of those who read it and dream about it.
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón, The Angel's Game

text: I love running. I’m not into marathons, but I am into avoiding problems at an accelerated rate.
author: Jarod Kintz, At even one penny, this book would be overpriced. In fact, free is too expensive, because you'd still waste time by reading it.

text: Hardships make or break people.
author: Margaret Mitchell, Gone with the Wind

text: Closed in a room, my imagination becomes the universe, and the rest of the world is missing out.
author: Criss Jami, Diotima, Battery, Electric Personality

text: Many people think excitement is happiness.... But when you are excited you are not peaceful. True happiness is based on peace.
author: Hanh Nhat Thich, The Art of Power

text: I was always holding onto people, and they were always leaving.
author: Lili St. Crow, Jealousy

text: Walk with the dreamers, the believers, the courageous, the cheerful, the planners, the doers, the successful people with their heads in the clouds and their feet on the ground. Let their spirit ignite a fire within you to leave this world better than when you found it...
author: Wilferd Peterson

text: Anybody who has survived his childhood has enough information about life to last him the rest of his days.
author: Flannery O'Connor, Mystery and Manners: Occasional Prose

text: A busy, vibrant, goal-oriented woman is so much more attractive than a woman who waits around for a man to validate her existence.
author: Mandy Hale, The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

text: Remember tonight... for it is the beginning of always
author: Dante Alighieri

text: I go to sleep alone, and wake up alone. I take walks. I work until I'm tired. I watch the wind play with the trash that's been under the snow all winter. Everything seems simple until you think about it. Why is love intensified by abscence?
author: Audrey Niffenegger, The Time Traveler's Wife

text: How could you go about choosing something that would hold the half of your heart you had to bury?
author: Jodi Picoult, Mercy

text: The essence of all beautiful art, all great art, is gratitude.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Love takes off masks that we fear we cannot live without and know we cannot live within.
author: James A. Baldwin

text: Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants.
author: Epictetus

text: It is not so much our friends' help that helps us, as the confidence of their help.
author: Epicurus

text: Oh, Lizzy! do anything rather than marry without affection.
author: Jane Austen, Pride and Prejudice

text: I loved you so much once. I did. More than anything in the whole wide world. Imagine that. What a laugh that is now. Can you believe it? We were so intimate once upon a time I can't believe it now. The memory of being that intimate with somebody. We were so intimate I could puke. I can't imagine ever being that intimate with somebody else. I haven't been.
author: Raymond Carver, Where I'm Calling From: New and Selected Stories

text: Your assumptions are your windows on the world. Scrub them off every once in a while, or the light won't come in.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: Write what disturbs you, what you fear, what you have not been willing to speak about. Be willing to be split open.
author: Natalie Goldberg, Writing Down the Bones: Freeing the Writer Within

text: The most fundamental aggression to ourselves, the most fundamental harm we can do to ourselves, is to remain ignorant by not having the courage and the respect to look at ourselves honestly and gently.
author: Pema Chödrön, When Things Fall Apart: Heart Advice for Difficult Times

text: We demand rigidly defined areas of doubt and uncertainty!
author: Douglas Adams, The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

text: Laws and principles are not for the times when there is no temptation: they are for such moments as this, when body and soul rise in mutiny against their rigour ... If at my convenience I might break them, what would be their worth?
author: Charlotte Brontë, Jane Eyre

text: Perhaps home is not a place but simply an irrevocable condition.
author: James Baldwin, Giovanni's Room

text: All I ask is one thing, and I’m asking this particularly of young people: please don’t be cynical. I hate cynicism, for the record, it’s my least favorite quality and it doesn’t lead anywhere. Nobody in life gets exactly what they thought they were going to get. But if you work really hard and you’re kind, amazing things will happen.
author: Conan O'Brien

text: To the people who love you, you are beautiful already. This is not because they’re blind to your shortcomings but because they so clearly see your soul. Your shortcomings then dim by comparison. The people who care about you are willing to let you be imperfect and beautiful, too. (20)
author: Victoria Moran, Lit From Within: Tending Your Soul For Lifelong Beauty

text: Nothing is at last sacred but the integrity of your own mind.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson, Self-Reliance

text: The authentic self is the soul made visible.
author: Sarah Ban Breathnach

text: People tend to be generous when sharing their nonsense, fear, and ignorance. And while they seem quite eager to feed you their negativity, please remember that sometimes the diet we need to be on is a spiritual and emotional one. Be cautious with what you feed your mind and soul. Fuel yourself with positivity and let that fuel propel you into positive action.
author: Steve Maraboli, Unapologetically You: Reflections on Life and the Human Experience

text: Happiness. Simple as a glass of chocolate or tortuous as the heart. Bitter. Sweet. Alive.
author: Joanne Harris, Chocolat

text: Don’t try to hog loneliness and keep it all to yourself. Share it with a special someone.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: We leave something of ourselves behind when we leave a place, we stay there, even though we go away. And there are things in us that we can find again only by going back there.
author: Pascal Mercier, Night Train to Lisbon

text: There you go...let it all slide out. Unhappiness can't stick in a person's soul when it's slick with tears.
author: Shannon Hale, Princess Academy

text: Reality exists in the human mind, and nowhere else.
author: George Orwell, 1984

text: Gym should be illegal. It's humiliating.
author: Laurie Halse Anderson, Speak

text: I am intrigued by the smile upon your face, and the sadness within your eyes
author: Jeremy Aldana

text: Sometimes the best and worst times of your life can coincide. It is a talent of the soul to discover the joy in pain—-thinking of moments you long for, and knowing you’ll never have them again. The beautiful ghosts of our past haunt us, and yet we still can’t decide if the pain they caused us out weighs the tender moments when they touched our soul. This is the irony of love.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: Step follows step,Hope follows Courage,Set your face towards danger,Set your heart on victory.
author: Gail Carson Levine, The Two Princesses of Bamarre

text: It's a helluva start, being able to recognize what makes you happy.
author: Lucille Ball

text: She hardly ever thought of him. He had worn a place for himself in some corner of her heart, as a sea shell, always boring against the rock, might do. The making of the place had been her pain. But now the shell was safely in the rock. It was lodged, and ground no longer.
author: T.H. White, The Once and Future King

text: Sometimes life is too hard to be alone, and sometimes life is too good to be alone.
author: Elizabeth Gilbert, Committed: A Skeptic Makes Peace with Marriage

text: How often do we tell our own life story? How often do we adjust, embellish, make sly cuts? And the longer life goes on, the fewer are those around to challenge our account, to remind us that our life is not our life, merely the story we have told about our life. Told to others, but—mainly—to ourselves.
author: Julian Barnes, The Sense of an Ending

text: Imagine there's no countriesIt isn't hard to doNothing to kill or die forAnd no religion tooImagine all the peopleLiving life in peaceYou may say that I'm a dreamerBut I'm not the only oneI hope someday you'll join usAnd the world will be as one
author: John Lennon, Imagine

text: The moment we begin to fear the opinions of others and hesitate to tell the truth that is in us, and from motives of policy are silent when we should speak, the divine floods of light and life no longer flow into our souls.
author: Elizabeth Cady Stanton

text: It is sometimes easier to be happy if you don't know everything.
author: Alexander McCall Smith, Morality for Beautiful Girls

text: Souls cross ages like clouds cross skies, an' tho' a cloud's shape nor hue nor size don't stay the same, it's still a cloud an' so is a soul. Who can say where the cloud's blowed from or who the soul'll be 'morrow? Only Sonmi the east an' the west an' the compass an' the atlas, yay, only the atlas o' clouds.
author: David Mitchell, Cloud Atlas

text: We cannot be sure of having something to live for unless we are willing to die for it.
author: Ernesto Che Guevara

text: You can't be brave if you've only had wonderful things happen to you.
author: Mary Tyler Moore

text: Before, I wanted to say: "I found love!"But now, I want to say: "I found a person. And he belongs to me and I belong to him.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: I was in the biggest breakdown of my life when I stopped crying long enough to let the words of my epiphany really sink in. That whore, karma, had finally made her way around, and had just bitch-slapped me right across the face. The realization only made me cry harder.
author: Jennifer Salaiz

text: When you feel someone else's pain and joy as powerfully as if it were your own, then you know you really loved them.
author: Ann Brashares, Girls In Pants: The Third Summer of the Sisterhood

text: Sometimes your light shines so bright that it blinds people from seeing who you really are.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: Don't walk in front of me, I may not follow. Don't walk behind me, I may not lead. There is only one happiness in life, to love and be loved.
author: George Sand

text: Love is like jumping out of an airplane with no parachute. But there’s no need to be frightened, because that plane is still on the ground.
author: Jarod Kintz, Love quotes for the ages. Specifically ages 18-81.

text: Relationships take up energy; letting go of them, psychiatrists theorize, entails mental work. When you lose someone you were close to, you have to reassess your picture of the world and your place in it. The more your identity was wrapped up with the deceased, the more difficult the loss.
author: Meghan O'Rourke

text: Anything that’s human is mentionable, and anything that is mentionable can be more manageable. When we can talk about our feelings, they become less overwhelming, less upsetting, and less scary. The people we trust with that important talk can help us know that we are not alone.
author: Fred Rogers

text: Unrequited love is the infinite curse of a lonely heart.
author: Christina Westover

text: Sometimes you break your heart in the right way, if you know what I mean.
author: Gregory David Roberts, Shantaram

text: Woman is not made to be the admiration of all, but the happiness of one.
author: Edmund Burke

text: Color is my day-long obsession, joy and torment.
author: Claude Monet

text: You will face your greatest opposition when you are closest to your biggest miracle.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: This time when we kiss, I feel it in the pit of my stomach, I feel it in my heart. And I realize love isn't about sex. It's about connection.
author: Ellen Hopkins

text: Your kids require you most of all to love them for who they are, not to spend your whole time trying to correct them.
author: Bill Ayers

text: It does not matter how long you are spending on the earth, how much money you have gathered or how much attention you have received. It is the amount of positive vibration you have radiated in life that matters,
author: Amit Ray, Meditation: Insights and Inspirations

text: It's no good trying to get rid of your own aloneness. You've got to stick to it all your life. Only at times, at times, the gap will be filled in. At times! But you have to wait for the times. Accept your own aloneness and stick to it, all your life. And then accept the times when the gap is filled in, when they come. But they've got to come. You can't force them.
author: D.H. Lawrence, Lady Chatterley's Lover

text: They've got no idea what happiness is, they don't know that without this love there is no happiness or unhappiness for us--there is no life.
author: Leo Tolstoy, Anna Karenina

text: Happiness is a perfume you cannot pour on others without getting some on yourself.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: And therein lies the whole of man's plight. Human time does not turn in a circle; it runs ahead in a straight line. That is why man cannot be happy: happiness is the longing for repetition.
author: Milan Kundera, The Unbearable Lightness of Being

text: Half a century ago, the amazing courage of Rosa Parks, the visionary leadership of Martin Luther King, and the inspirational actions of the civil rights movement led politicians to write equality into the law and make real the promise of America for all her citizens.
author: David Cameron

text: Love is too precious to be ashamed of.
author: Laurell K. Hamilton, A Stroke of Midnight

text: The boldness of his mind was sheathed in a scabbard of politeness.
author: Dumas Malone, Jefferson the Virginian

text: My past has not defined me, destroyed me, deterred me, or defeated me; it has only strengthened me.
author: Steve Maraboli, Unapologetically You: Reflections on Life and the Human Experience

text: Reputation is what others think of us; character is what God knows of us. When you have spent what feels like eternity trying to repair a few moments of time that destroyed the view others once had of you then you must ask yourself if you have the problem or is it really them? God doesn’t make us try so hard, only enemies do.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: How easy to be amiable in the midst of happiness and success.
author: William Ellery Channing

text: Intuition is the wisdom formed by feeling and instinct - a gift of knowing without reasoning... Belief is ignited by hope and supported by facts and evidence - it builds alignment and creates confidence. Belief is what sets energy in motion and creates the success that breeds more success.
author: Angela Ahrendts

text: right in this moment, I can´t even remember what unhappy feels like.
author: Maggie Stiefvater, Shiver

text: In the land of Gibberish, the man who makes sense, the man who speaks clearly, clearly speaks nonsense.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Life is filled with unanswered questions, but it is the courage to seek those answers that continues to give meaning to life. You can spend your life wallowing in despair, wondering why you were the one who was led towards the road strewn with pain, or you can be grateful that you are strong enough to survive it.
author: J.D. Stroube, Caged by Damnation

text: Just remember that the things you put into your head are there forever, he said. You might want to think about that.You forget some things, dont you?Yes. You forget what you want to remember and you remember what you want to forget.
author: Cormac McCarthy, The Road

text: She had loved him for such a long time, she thought. How was it that she did now know him at all?
author: Cassandra Clare, Clockwork Prince

text: Never let the future disturb you. You will meet it, if you have to, with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius, Meditations

text: To sell your soul is the easiest thing in the world. That's what everybody does every hour of his life. If I asked you to keep your soul - would you understand why that's much harder?
author: Ayn Rand, The Fountainhead

text: The trouble is that we have a bad habit, encouraged by pedants and sophisticates, of considering happiness as something rather stupid. Only pain is intellectual, only evil interesting. This is the treason of the artist; a refusal to admit the banality of evil and the terrible boredom of pain.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin, The Ones Who Walk Away from Omelas

text: I wish to weepbut sorrow isstupid.I wish to believebut belief is agraveyard.
author: Charles Bukowski, What Matters Most is How Well You Walk Through the Fire

text: I don't know what good it is to know so much and be smart as whips and all if it doesn't make you happy.
author: J.D. Salinger, Franny and Zooey

text: Have the courage to say no. Have the courage to face the truth. Do the right thing because it is right. These are the magic keys to living your life with integrity.
author: W. Clement Stone

text: I wish the Peace Corps and its volunteers continued success and perseverance. We are grateful for their contributions to society and dedication to providing assistance where it is needed. May the Peace Corps continue its legacy of service, both at home and abroad.
author: Kenny Marchant

text: Your soul knows the geography of your destiny. Your soul alone has the map of your future, therefore you can trust this indirect, oblique side of yourself. If you do, it will take you where you need to go, but more important it will teach you a kindness of rhythm in your journey.
author: John O'Donohue, Anam Cara: A Book of Celtic Wisdom

text: Be still, sad heart! and cease repining;Behind the clouds is the sun still shining;Thy fate is the common fate of all,Into each life some rain must fall
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, Ballads and Other Poems

text: And I didn't choose it, Kat. I chose you.
author: Ally Carter, Heist Society

text: It is an absolute human certainty that no one can know his own beauty or perceive a sense of his own worth until it has been reflected back to him in the mirror of another loving, caring human being.
author: John Joseph Powell, The Secret of Staying in Love

text: Sometimes I ask myself, "Do I have the courage to do the right thing when it matters most?"And that answer, I'm afraid, is silence.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Just because you are happy it does not mean that the day is perfect but that you have looked beyond its imperfections
author: Bob Marley

text: Let us dance in the sun, wearing wild flowers in our hair...
author: Susan Polis Schutz

text: Friendship is a strong and habitual inclination in two persons to promote the good and happiness of one another.
author: Eustace Budgell

text: Pointing to another world will never stop vice among us; shedding light over this world can alone help us.
author: Walt Whitman

text: I do not believe anyone can be perfectly well, who has a brain and a heart
author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow

text: You were put on this earth to achieve your greatest self, to live out your purpose, and to do it courageously.
author: Steve Maraboli, Life, the Truth, and Being Free

text: Falling in love is very real, but I used to shake my head when people talked about soul mates, poor deluded individuals grasping at some supernatural ideal not intended for mortals but sounded pretty in a poetry book. Then, we met, and everything changed, the cynic has become the converted, the sceptic, an ardent zealot.
author: E.A. Bucchianeri, Brushstrokes of a Gadfly

text: Love must not entreat,' she added, 'or demand. Love must have the strength to become certain within itself. Then it ceases merely to be attracted and begins to attract.
author: Hermann Hesse, Demian: Die Geschichte von Emil Sinclairs Jugend

text: Risk anything! Care no more for the opinion of others ... Do the hardest thing on earth for you. Act for yourself. Face the truth."(Journal entry, 14 October 1922)
author: Katherine Mansfield, Journal of Katherine Mansfield

text: Feathers filled the small room. Our laughter kept the feathers in the air. I thought about birds. Could they fly is there wasn't someone, somewhere, laughing?
author: Jonathan Safran Foer, Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close

text: As long as Man continues to be the ruthless destroyer of lower living beings, he will never know health or peace. For as long as men massacre animals, they will kill each other. Indeed, he who sows the seed of murder and pain cannot reap joy and love.
author: Pythagoras

text: Your naked body should only belong to those who fall in love with your naked soul.
author: Charlie Chaplin

text: I am my heart’s undertaker. Daily I go and retrieve its tattered remains, place them delicately into its little coffin, and bury it in the depths of my memory, only to have to do it all again tomorrow.
author: Emilie Autumn, The Asylum for Wayward Victorian Girls

text: Cassia.I know which life is my real one now, no matter what happens. It’s the one with you. For some reason, knowing that even one person knows my story makes things different. Maybe it’s like the poem says. Maybe this is my way of not going gentle.I love you. (Ky Markham)
author: Ally Condie, Matched

text: Far better it is to dare mighty things, to win glorious triumphs, even though checkered by failure, than to take rank with those poor spirits who neither enjoy much nor suffer much, because they live in the gray twilight that knows neither victory nor defeat.
author: Theodore Roosevelt, Strenuous Life

text: Power does not corrupt. Fear corrupts... perhaps the fear of a loss of power.
author: John Steinbeck

text: She had never imagined she had the power to make someone else so happy. And not a magical power, either--a purely human one.
author: Cassandra Clare, Clockwork Prince

text: If you love me as you say you do,' she whispered, 'make it so that I am at peace.
author: Leo Tolstoy, Anna Karenina

text: But she wasn’t around, and that’s the thing when your parents die, you feel like instead of going in to every fight with backup, you are going into every fight alone.
author: Mitch Albom, For One More Day

text: Never respond to an angry person with a fiery comeback, even if he deserves it...Don't allow his anger to become your anger.
author: Bohdi Sanders, Warrior Wisdom: Ageless Wisdom for the Modern Warrior

text: When you do something noble and beautiful and nobody noticed, do not be sad. For the sun every morning is a beautiful spectacle and yet most of the audience still sleeps.
author: John Lennon

text: We buy things we don't need with money we don't have to impress people we don't like.
author: Dave Ramsey, The Total Money Makeover: A Proven Plan for Financial Fitness

text: Most men claim to desire driven, independent and confident women. Yet when confronted with such a creature reverence often evolves into resent. For just like women, men need to be needed.
author: Tiffany Madison

text: Memory is the happiness of being alone.
author: Lois Lowry, Anastasia Krupnik

text: You're insane!"she shouted."Pretty cool, huh?""No!"Tally yelled. "Why didn't you tell me it was broken?"Shay shrugged. "More fun that way?""More fun?"Her heart beating fast,her vision strangely clear. She was full of anger and relief and...joy."Well, kind of. But you suck!
author: Scott Westerfeld, Uglies

text: The most enjoyable book in the world is the phone book, because think of all the sex that went into creating the content.
author: Jarod Kintz, 99 Cents For Some Nonsense

text: Obscurity and a competence—that is the life that is best worth living.
author: Mark Twain, Notebook

text: Only the development of compassion and understanding for others can bring us the tranquility and happiness we all seek.
author: Dalai Lama XIV

text: Love is that condition in which the happiness of another person is essential to your own... Jealousy is a disease, love is a healthy condition. The immature mind often mistakes one for the other, or assumes that the greater the love, the greater the jealousy.
author: Robert A. Heinlein

text: The works must be conceived with fire in the soul but executed with clinical coolness.
author: Joan Miro

text: True happiness arises, in the first place, from the enjoyment of one's self, and in the next, from the friendship and conversation of a few select companions.
author: Joseph Addison

text: Religion. A daughter of Hope and Fear, explaining to Ignorance the nature of the Unknowable.
author: Ambrose Bierce

text: What I'm not sure about, is if our lives have been so different from the lives of the people we save. We all complete. Maybe none of us really understand what we've lived through, or feel we've had enough time.
author: Kazuo Ishiguro, Never Let Me Go

text: I was a little excited but mostly blorft. "Blorft"is an adjective I just made up that means 'Completely overwhelmed but proceeding as if everything is fine and reacting to the stress with the torpor of a possum.' I have been blorft every day for the past seven years.
author: Tina Fey, Bossypants

text: Hope for love, pray for love, wish for love, dream for love…but don’t put your life on hold waiting for love.
author: Mandy Hale, The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

text: Sometimes I get real lonely sleeping with you.
author: Haruki Murakami, A Wild Sheep Chase

text: I define connection as the energy that exists between people when they feel seen, heard, and valued; when they can give and receive without judgment; and when they derive sustenance and strength from the relationship.
author: Brené Brown

text: Cherish those who seek the truth but beware of those who find it.
author: Voltaire

text: The only time you fail is when you fall down and stay down.
author: Stephen Richards, Cosmic Ordering: You can be successful

text: Art is unquestionably one of the purest and highest elements in human happiness. It trains the mind through the eye, and the eye through the mind. As the sun colors flowers, so does art color life.
author: John Lubbock, The Pleasures of Life

text: When you part from your friend, you grieve not;For that which you love most in him may be clearer in his absence, asthe mountain to the climber is clearer from the plain.
author: Kahlil Gibran, The Prophet

text: O may I join the choir invisibleOf those immortal dead who live againIn minds made better by their presence; liveIn pulses stirred to generosity,In deeds of daring rectitude...
author: George Eliot, O May I Join the Choir Invisible! And Other Favourite Poems

text: Impressionism is the newspaper of the soul.
author: Henri Matisse

text: Love feels no burden, thinks nothing of trouble, attempts what is above its strength, pleads no excuse of impossibility; for it thinks all things lawful for itself, and all things possible.
author: Thomas a Kempis

text: Humanity does not ask us to be happy. It merely asks us to be brilliant on its behalf.
author: Orson Scott Card, Ender's Game

text: A man says a lot of things in summer he doesn't mean in winter.
author: Patricia Briggs, Dragon Blood

text: More people are leaving TV behind to read my books than ever before. In the last year alone I gained over two readers (three, to be exact). So I’d like to take a moment and say thanks mom, dad, and kidnap victim I keep chained in the basement.
author: Jarod Kintz, The Days of Yay are Here! Wake Me Up When They're Over.

text: To achieve great things, two things are needed: a plan and not quite enough time.
author: Leonard Bernstein

text: In order to rise from its own ashes, a Phoenix first must burn.
author: Octavia E. Butler

text: We're all golden sunflowers inside.
author: Allen Ginsberg

text: You have to participate relentlessly in the manifestation of your own blessings.
author: Elizabeth Gilbert

text: People wait around too long for love. I'm happy with all of my lusts!
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: When you miss someone....it’s weird…your body doesn’t function normally..as it should. Because I miss you, and my heart…it’s not steady…my soul it sings numb. Fingers are cold…like you…your soul.
author: Coco J. Ginger

text: I have so much in me, and the feeling for her absorbs it all; I have so much, and without her it all comes to nothing.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, The Sorrows of Young Werther

text: May Light always surround you;Hope kindle and rebound you.May your Hurts turn to Healing;Your Heart embrace Feeling.May Wounds become Wisdom;Every Kindness a Prism.May Laughter infect you;Your Passion resurrect you.May Goodness inspire your Deepest Desires.Through all that you Reach For, May your arms Never Tire.
author: D. Simone

text: Music is an outburst of the soul.
author: Frederick Delius

text: Is life not a thousand times too short for us to bore ourselves?
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Somehow I can't believe that there are any heights that can't be scaled by a man who knows the secrets of making dreams come true. This special secret, it seems to me, can be summarized in four Cs. They are curiosity, confidence, courage, and constancy, and the greatest of all is confidence. When you believe in a thing, believe in it all the way, implicitly and unquestionable.
author: Walt Disney Company

text: Every woman that finally figured out her worth, has picked up her suitcases of pride and boarded a flight to freedom, which landed in the valley of change.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: The reason many people in our society are miserable, sick, and highly stressed is because of an unhealthy attachment to things they have no control over.
author: Steve Maraboli, Unapologetically You: Reflections on Life and the Human Experience

text: Happiness was but the occasional episode in a general drama of pain.
author: Thomas Hardy, The Mayor of Casterbridge

text: Friendship improves happiness and abates misery, by the doubling of our joy and the dividing of our grief.
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

text: People tend to think that happiness is a stroke of luck, something that will descend like fine weather if you're fortunate. But happiness is the result of personal effort. You fight for it, strive for it, insist upon it, and sometimes even travel around the world looking for it. You have to participate relentlessly.
author: Elizabeth Gilbert

text: I think that many things that go on in an art school have a tendency to undermine confidence, and that shouldn't be part of the ballgame, ever.
author: Lewis Black

text: ...Nobody knows like a woman how to say things that are both sweet and profound. Sweetness and depth, this is all of woman; this is Heaven.
author: Victor Hugo, Les Misérables

text: Don't give up the fight,Stand up for your rights.
author: Bob Marley

text: You know all that sympathy that you feel for an abused child who suffers without a good mom or dad to love and care for them? Well, they don't stay children forever. No one magically becomes an adult the day they turn eighteen. Some people grow up sooner, many grow up later. Some never really do. But just remember that some people in this world are older versions of those same kids we cry for.
author: Ashly Lorenzana

text: Minds are like flowers, they only open when the time is right.
author: Stephen Richards

text: Each of us has his own rhythm of suffering.
author: Roland Barthes

text: The trick is in what one emphasizes. We either make ourselves miserable, or we make ourselves happy. The amount of work is the same.
author: Carlos Castaneda

text: My brain is divided into two butterflies, and both are in love with your rose-shaped heart. If you've got the garden, I've got my whole life.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Right,"she said, "We're going to the Land of the Dead and I shouldn't think negative.
author: Rick Riordan, The Lightning Thief

text: We pass through this world but once. Few tragedies can be more extensive than the stunting of life, few injustices deeper than the denial of an opportunity to strive or even to hope, by a limit imposed from without, but falsely identified as lying within.
author: Stephen Jay Gould, The Mismeasure of Man

text: I believe compassion to be one of the few things we can practice that will bring immediate and long-term happiness to our lives. I’m not talking about the short-term gratification of pleasures like sex, drugs or gambling (though I’m not knocking them), but something that will bring true and lasting happiness. The kind that sticks.
author: Dalai Lama XIV

text: There is only one way to happiness and that is to cease worrying about things which are beyond the power or our will.
author: Epictetus

text: I wore your promise on my finger for one yearI'll wear your name on my heart til I dieBecause you were my boy, you were my only boy forever.
author: Coco J. Ginger

text: Money may not buy happiness, but I'd rather cry in a Jaguar than on a bus.
author: Françoise Sagan

text: But maybe happiness isn't in the choosing. Maybe it's in the fiction, in the pretending: that wherever we have ended up is where we intended to be all along.
author: Lauren Oliver, Requiem

text: Expectations make people miserable, so whatever yours are, lower them. You'll definitely be happier.
author: Simone Elkeles, How to Ruin Your Boyfriend's Reputation

text: I would like this to be a new day. I would hope now that we can focus on doing the people's business.
author: Ernie Fletcher

text: I'm just thankful for everything, all the blessings in my life, trying to stay that way. I think that's the best way to start your day and finish your day. It keeps everything in perspective.
author: Tim Tebow

text: There are two ways to be happy: improve your reality, or lower your expectations.
author: Jodi Picoult, Nineteen Minutes

text: The most merciful thing in the world, I think, is the inability of the human mind to correlate all its contents... some day the piecing together of dissociated knowledge will open up such terrifying vistas of reality, and of our frightful position therein, that we shall either go mad from the revelation or flee from the light into the peace and safety of a new Dark Age.
author: H.P. Lovecraft

text: Unreturned love is like trying to make a sandwich with one slice of bread. Don’t stress—fold it in half and love yourself.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Ever since happiness heard your name, it has been running through the streets trying to find you.
author: حافظ

text: Later she remembered all the hours of the afternoon as happy -- one of those uneventful times that seem at the moment only a link between past and future pleasure, but turn out to have been the pleasure itself.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald, Tender is the Night

text: Scars are just another kind of memory.
author: M.L. Stedman, The Light Between Oceans

text: I have outlasted all desire,My dreams and I have grown apart;My grief alone is left entire,The gleamings of an empty heart.The storms of ruthless dispensationHave struck my flowery garland numb,I live in lonely desolationAnd wonder when my end will come.Thus on a naked tree-limb, blastedBy tardy winter's whistling chill,A single leaf which has outlastedIts season will be trembling still.
author: Alexander Pushkin

text: I'm still trying to figure out how to write about cancer and my family's experience with it. If I had been able to write 'The Pura Principle' back in those days, I'm positive it would have had no humor in it. Which means the story would have been false.
author: Junot Diaz

text: She has a sly smile, and eyes that seem to see my essence as they explore my soul and implore my spirit to enter her. I look at her and I see love.
author: Jarod Kintz, Love quotes for the ages. Specifically ages 18-81.

text: I’ll be your Dostoevsky, if you’ll be my Tolstoy. Our life together will be so full of despair that death will be like a gulag full of joy.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: My soul is a hidden orchestra; I know not what instruments, what fiddlestrings and harps, drums and tamboura I sound and clash inside myself. All I hear is the symphony.
author: Fernando Pessoa, The Book of Disquiet

text: I don't understand the point of being together if you're not the happiest.
author: Gillian Flynn, Gone Girl

text: One swallow does not make a summer,neither does one fine day; similarly one day or brief time of happiness does not make a person entirely happy.
author: Aristotle, The Nicomachean Ethics

text: Of all the seasons, winter is the most conducive to the great art of dormancy. This art requires an appreciation of semi-consciousness: the beautiful and necessary prelude to sleep - a special pleasure in itself that is all too often neglected, under-valued or looked down upon.
author: Michael Leunig

text: Life is too short to worry about anything. You had better enjoy it because the next day promises nothing.
author: Eric Davis

text: Your success and happiness lies in you. Resolve to keep happy, and your joy and you shall form an invincible host against difficulties.
author: Helen Keller

text: Sweet is the memory of distant friends! Like the mellow rays of the departing sun, it falls tenderly, yet sadly, on the heart.
author: Washington Irving

text: I've crossed some kind of invisible line. I feel as if I've come to a place I never thought I'd have to come to. And I don't know how I got here. It's a strange place. It's a place where a little harmless dreaming and then some sleepy, early-morning talk has led me into considerations of death and annihilation.
author: Raymond Carver, Where I'm Calling From: New and Selected Stories

text: You are essentially who you create yourself to be and all that occurs in your life is the result of your own making.
author: Stephen Richards, Think Your way to Success: Let Your Dreams Run Free

text: Happiness doesn't result from what we get, but from what we give.
author: Ben Carson

text: The world is so empty if one thinks only of mountains, rivers & cities; but to know someone who thinks & feels with us, & who, though distant, is close to us in spirit, this makes the earth for us an inhabited garden.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: It's funny how, in this journey of life, even though we may begin at different times and places, our paths cross with others so that we may share our love, compassion, observations, and hope. This is a design of God that I appreciate and cherish.
author: Steve Maraboli, Unapologetically You: Reflections on Life and the Human Experience

text: Happiness is not achieved by the conscious pursuit of happiness; it is generally the by-product of other activities.
author: Aldous Huxley

text: Youth offers the promise of happiness, but life offers the realities of grief.
author: Nicholas Sparks, The Rescue

text: Spending time with God is the key to our strength and success in all areas of life. Be sure that you never try to work God into your schedule, but always work your schedule around Him.
author: Joyce Meyer

text: Only priests and fools are fearless and I've never been on the best of terms with God.
author: Patrick Rothfuss, The Name of the Wind

text: Nobody ever gets what they want when it comes to love.
author: Scott Turow

text: Death leaves a heartache no one can heal, love leaves a memory no one can steal.From an Irish headstone
author: Richard Puz, The Carolinian

text: I want to get the words "Courage"and "Bravery"tattooed across my back, so people could associate me with those things as they read them while they chase me.
author: Jarod Kintz, I Want

text: Dance. Smile. Giggle. Marvel. TRUST. HOPE. LOVE. WISH. BELIEVE. Most of all, enjoy every moment of the journey, and appreciate where you are at this moment instead of always focusing on how far you have to go.
author: Mandy Hale, The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

text: If you have a dream, don’t just sit there. Gather courage to believe that you can succeed and leave no stone unturned to make it a reality.
author: Roopleen

text: The words ‘I Love You’ kill, and resurrect millions, in less than a second.
author: Aberjhani, Elemental: The Power of Illuminated Love

text: Self-respect is the root of discipline: The sense of dignity growswith the ability to say no to oneself.
author: Abraham Joshua Heschel

text: Courage, my friends; 'tis not too late to build a better world.
author: Tommy Douglas

text: Pierre was right when he said that one must believe in the possibility of happiness in order to be happy, and I now believe in it. Let the dead bury the dead, but while I'm alive, I must live and be happy.
author: Leo Tolstoy, War and Peace

text: She had become accustomed to being lonely. She was used to walking alone and to being considered 'different.' She did not suffer too much.
author: Betty Smith, A Tree Grows in Brooklyn

text: Our bodies have five senses: touch, smell, taste, sight, hearing. But not to be overlooked are the senses of our souls: intuition, peace, foresight, trust, empathy. The differences between people lie in their use of these senses; most people don't know anything about the inner senses while a few people rely on them just as they rely on their physical senses, and in fact probably even more.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: ...the opposite of love is not hate -- it's apathy. It's not giving a damn. If somebody hates me, they must "feel"something ... or they couldn't possibly hate. Therefore, there's some way in which I can get to them.
author: Leo Buscaglia

text: For a day, just for one day,Talk about that which disturbs no oneAnd bring some peace into yourBeautiful eyes.
author: حافظ, The Subject Tonight Is Love: 60 Wild and Sweet Poems of Hafiz

text: What greater thing is there for two human souls, than to feel that they are joined for life--to strengthen each other in all labor, to rest on each other in all sorrow, to minister to each other in all pain, to be one with each other in silent unspeakable memories at the moment of the last parting?
author: George Eliot, Adam Bede

text: There are some things one remembers even though they may never have happened.
author: Harold Pinter, Old Times

text: Everyone grieves in different ways. For some, it could take longer or shorter. I do know it never disappears. An ember still smolders inside me. Most days, I don’t notice it, but, out of the blue, it’ll flare to life.
author: Maria V. Snyder, Storm Glass

text: Freddie experienced the sort of abysmal soul-sadness which afflicts one of Tolstoy's Russian peasants when, after putting in a heavy day's work strangling his father, beating his wife, and dropping the baby into the city's reservoir, he turns to the cupboards, only to find the vodka bottle empty.
author: P.G. Wodehouse, The Best of Wodehouse: An Anthology

text: When you make a choice, you change the future.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: Some books are undeservedly forgotten; none are undeservedly remembered.
author: W.H. Auden, The Dyer's Hand

text: Half the night I waste in sighs,Half in dreams I sorrow afterThe delight of early skies;In a wakeful dose I sorrowFor the hand, the lips, the eyes,For the meeting of the morrow,The delight of happy laughter,The delight of low replies.
author: Alfred Lord Tennyson, Maud, and other poems

text: It has shown me that everything is illuminated in the light of the past. It is always along the side of us...on the inside, looking out.
author: Jonathan Safran Foer

text: Childhood should be carefree, playing in the sun; not living a nightmare in the darkness of the soul.
author: Dave Pelzer, A Child Called "It"

text: There is no beauty in sadness. No honor in suffering. No growth in fear. No relief in hate. It’s just a waste of perfectly good happiness.
author: Katerina Stoykova Klemer

text: The Uses Of Sorrow(In my sleep I dreamed this poem)Someone I loved once gave mea box full of darkness.It took me years to understandthat this, too, was a gift.
author: Mary Oliver, Thirst

text: I love sleepwalking, because when else would I get to combine exercise and rest?
author: Jarod Kintz, The Merits of Marthaism, and How Being Named Susan Can Benefit You

text: Every moment and every event of every man's life on earth plants something in his soul.
author: Thomas Merton

text: And I'll dance with you in Vienna,I'll be wearing a river's disguise.The hyacinth wild on my shouldermy mouth on the dew of your thighs.And I'll bury my soul in a scrapbook,with the photographs there and the moss.And I'll yield to the flood of your beauty,my cheap violin and my cross.
author: Leonard Cohen, Stranger Music: Selected Poems and Songs

text: Frustration and Love can’t exist in the same place at the same time, so get real and start doing what you would rather be doing in life. Love your life. All of it. Even the heavy shit that happened to you when you were 8. All of it was and IS perfect.
author: Jason Mraz

text: Love is made up of three unconditional properties in equal measure:1. Acceptance2. Understanding3. AppreciationRemove any one of the three and the triangle falls apart.Which, by the way, is something highly inadvisable. Think about it — do you really want to live in a world of only two dimensions?So, for the love of a triangle, please keep love whole.
author: Vera Nazarian, The Perpetual Calendar of Inspiration

text: Stories make us more alive, more human, more courageous, more loving.
author: Madeleine L'Engle

text: The center will hold.” “How do you know?” “Because we are the center.
author: Richelle Mead, The Fiery Heart

text: It’s the fire in my eyes, And the flash of my teeth, The swing in my waist, And the joy in my feet. I’m a woman Phenomenally.
author: Maya Angelou, Phenomenal Woman: Four Poems Celebrating Women

text: I don’t really want to become normal, average, standard. I want merely to gain in strength, in the courage to live out my life more fully, enjoy more, experience more. I want to develop even more original and more unconventional traits
author: Anaïs Nin, The Diary of Anaïs Nin, Vol. 1: 1931-1934

text: My God, a moment of bliss. Why, isn't that enough for a whole lifetime?
author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky, White Nights

text: Sometimes you weren't supposed to share pain. Sometimes it was best just to deal with it alone.
author: Sarah Addison Allen, The Sugar Queen

text: And if you couldn't be loved, the next best thing was to be let alone.
author: L.M. Montgomery

text: One should use common words to say uncommon things
author: Arthur Schopenhauer

text: I am terrified by this dark thing that sleeps in me.
author: Sylvia Plath, The Collected Poems

text: The mind can calculate, but the spirit yearns, and the heart knows what the heart knows
author: Stephen King

text: The secret of happiness is not in doing what one likes, but in liking what one does.
author: J.M. Barrie

text: If you die you're completely happy and your soul somewhere lives on. I'm not afraid of dying. Total peace after death, becoming someone else is the best hope I've got.
author: Kurt Cobain

text: Believe in yourself and there will come a day when others will have no choice but to believe with you.
author: Cynthia Kersey

text: My expectations were reduced to zero when I was 21. Everything since then has been a bonus."[The Science of Second-Guessing (New York Times Magazine Interview, December 12, 2004)]
author: Stephen Hawking

text: If you have any young friends who aspire to become writers, the second greatest favor you can do them is to present them with copies of The Elements of Style. The first greatest, of course, is to shoot them now, while they’re happy.
author: Dorothy Parker

text: At that moment I had no mind to change, or not change, or throw against the nearest wall.
author: James Patterson

text: As long as I am breathing, in my eyes, I am just beginning.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: There will always be someone willing to hurt you, put you down, gossip about you, belittle your accomplishments and judge your soul. It is a fact that we all must face. However, if you realize that God is a best friend that stands beside you when others cast stones you will never be afraid, never feel worthless and never feel alone.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: Lonely people tend, rather, to be lonely because they decline to bear the psychic costs of being around other humans. They are allergic to people. People affect them too strongly.
author: David Foster Wallace, A Supposedly Fun Thing I'll Never Do Again: Essays and Arguments

text: Always forgive, but never forget, else you will be a prisoner of your own hatred, and doomed to repeat your mistakes forever.
author: Wil Zeus, Sun Beyond the Clouds

text: My friends, love is better than anger. Hope is better than fear. Optimism is better than despair. So let us be loving, hopeful and optimistic. And we'll change the world.
author: Jack Layton

text: How would your life be different if…You stopped worrying about things you can’t control and started focusing on the things you can? Let today be the day…You free yourself from fruitless worry, seize the day and take effective action on things you can change.
author: Steve Maraboli, Life, the Truth, and Being Free

text: it is impossible to build one's own happiness on the unhappiness of others. This perspective is at the heart of Buddhist teachings.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text: I love you. I love you. I send this message through my fingers and into his, up his arm and into his heart. Hear me. I love you. And I'm sorry to leave you.
author: Jenny Downham, Before I Die

text: If you learn to really sit with loneliness and embrace it for the gift that it is…an opportunity to get to know YOU, to learn how strong you really are, to depend on no one but YOU for your happiness…you will realize that a little loneliness goes a LONG way in creating a richer, deeper, more vibrant and colorful YOU.
author: Mandy Hale, The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

text: I wondered how many people there were in the world who suffered, and continued to suffer, because they could not break out from their own web of shyness and reserve, and in their blindness and folly built up a great distorted wall in front of them that hid the truth.
author: Daphne du Maurier, Rebecca

text: I drank her essence, and it’s like she never existed and now I’m thirsty again. Let this be a lesson in love.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: There comes a time in your life when you have to choose to turn the page, write another book or simply close it.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: We are all wonderful, beautiful wrecks. That's what connects us--that we're all broken, all beautifully imperfect.
author: Emilio Estevez

text: Happiness, you see, its just an illusion of Fate, a heavenly sleight of hand designed to make you believe in fairy tales. But there's no happily ever after. You'll only find happy endings in books. Some books.
author: Ellen Hopkins

text: The true measure of success is how many times you can bounce back from failure.
author: Stephen Richards

text: The terrorists thought they would change my aims and stop my ambitions, but nothing changed in my life except this: weakness, fear and hopelessness died. Strength, power and courage were born.
author: Malala Yousafzai

text: It’s not that we have to quit this life one day, but it’s how many things we have to quit all at once: music, laughter,the physics of falling leaves, automobiles, holding hands,the scent of rain, the concept of subway trains... if only one could leave this life slowly!
author: Roman Payne, Rooftop Soliloquy

text: To be stupid, and selfish, and to have good health are the three requirements for happiness - though if stupidity is lacking, the others are useless.
author: Julian Barnes, Flaubert's Parrot

text: and when all the wars are over, a butterfly will still be beautiful.
author: Ruskin Bond, Scenes from a Writer's Life

text: It’s always hard to lose somebody. It leaves a hole in you heart that never grows back.
author: Kevin Brooks, Lucas

text: I had a dream about you. No words were exchanged, but we spoke with our eyes. My eyes said, “I love you,” while your eyes told me, “I’m asleep.” You always were more romantic than me.
author: Jarod Kintz, I had a dream about you 2

text: I just looked at her, feeling utterly empty. I didn’t know what I was supposed to say to her. My life is in that bed. Please let me stay.
author: Maggie Stiefvater, Linger

text: I used to think how lucky she was to have me, but now I realize that’s backwards. A spirit of gratitude yields patience, love, and forgiveness.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: No one who achieves success does so without acknowledging the help of others. The wise and confident acknowledge this help with gratitude.
author: Alfred North Whitehead

text: I am happy to say that everyone that I have met in my life, I have gained something from them; be it negative or positive, it has enforced and reinforced my life in some aspect.
author: Walter Payton

text: I hope you live a life you’re proud of. If you find that you’re not, I hope you have the strength to start all over again.
author: Eric Roth, The Curious Case of Benjamin Button Screenplay

text: We are all of us born with a letter inside us, and that only if we are true to ourselves, may we be allowed to read it before we die.
author: Douglas Coupland

text: Reading is that fruitful miracle of a communication in the midst of solitude.
author: Marcel Proust

text: In times of stress, the best thing we can do for each other is to listen with our ears and our hearts and to be assured that our questions are just as important as our answers.
author: Fred Rogers, The World According to Mister Rogers: Important Things to Remember

text: It is only through mystery and madness that the soul is revealed
author: Thomas Moore

text: She can't help it,' he said. 'She's got the soul of a poet and the emotional makeup of a junkyard dog.
author: Stephen King, Under the Dome

text: Death, therefore, the most awful of evils, is nothing to us, seeing that, when we are, death is not come, and, when death is come, we are not.
author: Epicurus

text: I drank so much booze I was bamboozled. Alcohol makes my mind as discombobulated as love makes my heart. I’d sure appreciate it if you poured me a large glass of romance.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Being single doesn't mean you're weak, it means that you're strong enough to wait for the right person.
author: Niall Horan

text: Hide things everywhere. Forget about them. Find them randomly and feel surprised like a pirate finding buried treasure. Avoid scurvy. Love more.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: You take away all the other luxuries in life, and if you can make someone smile and laugh, you have given the most special gift: happiness.
author: Brad Garrett

text: There is emotion in the hug, and there is respect and a form of love. Emotion that comes from honesty, respect that comes from challenge, and the form of love that exists between people whose minds have touched, whose hearts have touched, whose souls have touched. Our minds touched. Our hearts touched. Our souls touched.We separate.
author: James Frey, A Million Little Pieces

text: I make love with a focus and intensity that most people reserve for sleep.
author: Dark Jar Tin Zoo, Love Quotes for the Ages. Specifically Ages 19-91.

text: Even a soul submerged in sleep is hard at work and helps make something of the world.
author: Heraclitus, Fragments

text: My I love you was a measured response, like one gallon of coffee in one cup of sugar. It was just overflowing with an awakening of my soul.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: What sunshine is to flowers, smiles are to humanity. These are but trifles, to be sure; but scattered along life's pathway, the good they do is inconceivable.
author: Joseph Addison

text: There are random moments - tossing a salad, coming up the driveway to the house, ironing the seams flat on a quilt square, standing at the kitchen window and looking out at the delphiniums, hearing a burst of laughter from one of my children's rooms - when I feel a wavelike rush of joy. This is my true religion: arbitrary moments of of nearly painful happiness for a life I feel privileged to lead.
author: Elizabeth Berg, The Art of Mending

text: ...when pain is over, the remembrance of it often becomes a pleasure.
author: Jane Austen, Persuasion

text: Death, so called, is a thing which makes men weep, And yet a third of life is passed in sleep.
author: George Gordon Byron

text: Life is a bowl of cherries. Some cherries are rotten while others are good; its your job to throw out the rotten ones and forget about them while you enjoy eating the ones that are good! There are two kinds of people: those who choose to throw out the good cherries and wallow in all the rotten ones, and those who choose to throw out all the rotten ones and savor all the good ones.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: Two things you will never have to chase: True friends & true love.
author: Mandy Hale

text: Be courteous to all, but intimate with few, and let those few be well tried before you give them your confidence.
author: George Washington

text: The baby batScreamed out in fright,'Turn on the dark,I'm afraid of the light.
author: Shel Silverstein

text: My heart has joined the Thousand, for my friend stopped running today.
author: Richard Adams, Watership Down

text: If you need me I’ll be napping in the supplies closet. The most important part of an attack is the planning.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Your battles inspired me - not the obvious material battles but those that were fought and won behind your forehead.
author: James Joyce

text: I got a zero. That’s like a perfect ten—it’s only one away from being flawless. Remember this next time you think you have nothing.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: The middle finger is a great body language tool for letting people know you’re upset. Poetry isn’t quite as effective in a fit of road rage.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: The invariable mark of wisdom is to see the miraculous in the common.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: I really think I write about everyday life. I don't think I'm quite as odd as others say I am. Life is intrinsically, well, boring and dangerous at the same time. At any given moment the floor may open up. Of course, it almost never does; that's what makes it so boring.
author: Edward Gorey

text: I don't know why people are afraid of lust. Then I can imagine that they are very afraid of me, for I have a great lust for everything. A lust for life, a lust for how the summer-heated street feels beneath my feet, a lust for the touch of another's skin on my skin...a lust for everything. I even lust after cake. Yes, I am very lusty and very scary.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: I had a dream about you. You were storing my brain in a pickle jar in the fridge, and I only discovered it when I went to garnish my hamburger. Mindless and hungry, I was a US politician’s ideal voter.
author: Jarod Kintz, We Had A #Dream About You

text: As an author, I like self-help, because clapping can be done by myself, for myself. I should buy gloves.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: The world’s bumper sticker reads: Life sucks, and then you die. Perhaps Christian bumper stickers should read: Life sucks, but then you find hope and you can’t wait to die.
author: Ted Dekker, The Slumber of Christianity: Awakening a Passion for Heaven on Earth

text: The person who tries to live alone will not succeed as a human being. His heart withers if it does not answer another heart. His mind shrinks away if he hears only the echoes of his own thoughts and finds no other inspiration.
author: Pearl S. Buck

text: I am a Mormon because I love the Lord Jesus Christ. And as I exercise faith in Him, I am blessed with the Holy Spirit in my life.
author: Jane Clayson

text: So I learned two things that night, and the next day, from him: the perfection of a moment, and the fleeting nature of it.
author: Margaret George, Memoirs of Cleopatra

text: He is terribly afraid of dying because he hasn’t yet lived.
author: Franz Kafka

text: You say a new era in art is preparing; you sensed it coming; continue your studies without weakening. God will do the rest.
author: Paul Cezanne

text: True regret is knowing you missed your only opportunity to be simply a good friend to someone that was exactly like you.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: . . . owning a dog always ended with this sadness because dogs just don't live as long as people do.
author: John Grogan, Marley and Me: Life and Love With the World's Worst Dog

text: Until you make peace with who you are, you'll never be content with what you have.
author: Doris Mortman

text: Remember my friend, that knowledge is stronger than memory, and we should not trust the weaker
author: Bram Stoker, DRACULA

text: I think people believe in heaven because they don't like the idea of dying, because they want to carry on living and they don't like the idea that other people will move into their house and put their things into the rubbish.
author: Mark Haddon, The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time

text: A melancholy-looking man, he had the appearance of one who has searched for the leak in life's gas-pipe with a lighted candle.
author: P.G. Wodehouse, The Man Upstairs and Other Stories

text: If there was a God. I would spit in his face for subjecting me to this. If there was a Devil, I would sell my sould to make it end. If there was something Higher that controlled out f***ing fates, I would tell it to take my fate and shove it up its fucking ass. Shove it hard and far, you motherf***er. Please end. Please end. Please end.
author: James Frey, A Million Little Pieces

text: Her and I, we passed each other, and now she’s in my past. That’s one present I’d like to unwrap in the future.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: The battle you are going through is not fueled by the words or actions of others; it is fueled by the mind that gives it importance.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: Reading well is one of the great pleasures that solitude can afford you.
author: Harold Bloom

text: We must never stop dreaming. Dreams provide nourishment for the soul, just as a meal does for the body.
author: Paulo Coelho, The Pilgrimage

text: I belong, and my penis, it be long.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: My heart only grows more powerful the more my flowing love goes unabsorbed, unobserved, and unappreciated.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: But hurry, let's entwine ourselves as one, our mouth broken, our soul bitten by love, so time discovers us safely destroyed.
author: Federico García Lorca

text: [F]or just one second, look at your life and see how perfect it is. Stop looking for the next secret door that is going to lead you to your real life. Stop waiting. This is it: there's nothing else. It's here, and you'd better decide to enjoy it or you're going to be miserable wherever you go, for the rest of your life, forever.
author: Lev Grossman, The Magicians

text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it. (21)
author: Hanh Nhat Thich, Peace Is Every Step: The Path of Mindfulness in Everyday Life

text: Looking at beauty in the world, is the first step of purifying the mind.
author: Amit Ray, Meditation: Insights and Inspirations

text: The discontent and frustration that you feel is entirely your own creation.
author: Stephen Richards, Think Your way to Success: Let Your Dreams Run Free

text: Re-examine all you have been told. Dismiss what insults your soul.
author: Walt Whitman

text: He felt now that he was not simply close to her, but that he did not know where he ended and she began.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: There are three deaths. The first is when the body ceases to function. The second is when the body is consigned to the grave. The third is that moment, sometime in the future, when your name is spoken for the last time.
author: David Eagleman, Sum: Forty Tales from the Afterlives

text: Seize the moments of happiness, love and be loved! That is the only reality in the world, all else is folly. It is the one thing we are interested in here.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: I remember her words about remembering my words, and that’s when it hit me: the quickest way to scalpel open a heart is with a declaration of love.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: An attitude of positive expectation is the mark of the superior personality.
author: Brian Tracy

text: It is a happiness to wonder; -- it is a happiness to dream.
author: Edgar Allan Poe, Complete Stories and Poems of Edgar Allen Poe

text: I need solitude for my writing; not 'like a hermit' - that wouldn't be enough - but like a dead man.
author: Franz Kafka

text: My imagination makes me human and makes me a fool; it gives me all the world and exiles me from it.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin

text: I suffer from life and from other people. I can’t look at reality face to face. Even the sun discourages and depresses me. Only at night and all alone, withdrawn, forgotten and lost, with no connection to anything real or useful — only then do I find myself and feel comforted.
author: Fernando Pessoa, The Book of Disquiet

text: I slept and I dreamed that life is all joy. I woke and I saw that life is all service. I served and I saw that service is joy.
author: Kahlil Gibran

text: You have been told that, even like a chain, you are as weak as your weakest link.This is but half the truth.You are also as strong as your strongest link.To measure you by your smallest deed is to reckon the power of the oceanby the frailty of its foam.To judge you by your failures is to cast blame upon the seasons for their inconstancy.
author: Kahlil Gibran, The Prophet

text: Remembering. Forgetting. I'm not sure which is worse.
author: Kelley Armstrong, The Calling

text: I can safely say, on the authority of all that is revealed in the Word of God, that any man or woman on this earth who is bored and turned off by worship is not ready for heaven.
author: A.W. Tozer

text: In this life, we have to make many choices. Some are very important choices. Some are not. Many of our choices are between good and evil. The choices we make, however, determine to a large extent our happiness or our unhappiness, because we have to live with the consequences of our choices.
author: James E. Faust

text: You, of all people, deserve a happy ending. Despite everything that happened to you, you aren't bitter. You aren't cold. You've just retreated a little and been shy, and that's okay. If I were a fairy godmother, I would give you your heart's desire in an istant. And I would wipe away your tears and tell you not to cry.-Rachel to Julia
author: Sylvain Reynard, Gabriel's Inferno

text: Drink my Distraction Juice (not from concentrate). It tastes like love, only not so focused on just one ingredient.
author: Jarod Kintz, Love quotes for the ages. Specifically ages 18-81.

text: He was afraid of defiling the love which filled his soul.
author: Leo Tolstoy, Anna Karenina

text: I believe that every single event in life happens in an opportunity to choose love over fear.
author: Oprah Winfrey

text: If we had no winter, the spring would not be so pleasant: if we did not sometimes taste of adversity, prosperity would not be so welcome."[Meditations Divine and Moral]
author: Anne Bradstreet, The Works of Anne Bradstreet

text: He looks at her and smiles. "You're sort of dangerous, you know?"She stares at him. "Me?""Yeah,"he says sitting back. "I'm way too honest with you.
author: Jennifer E. Smith, The Statistical Probability of Love at First Sight

text: Joy descends gently upon us like the evening dew, and does not patter down like a hailstorm.
author: Jean Paul

text: Happiness comes from within. It is not dependent on external things or on other people. You become vulnerable and can be easily hurt when your feelings of security and happiness depend on the behavior and actions of other people. Never give your power to anyone else.
author: Brian L. Weiss

text: Growing old is mandatory. Growing up is optional.
author: Carroll Bryant

text: You don't ask people with knives in their stomachs what would make them happy; happiness is no longer the point. It's all about survival; it's all about whether you pull the knife out and bleed to death or keep it in...
author: Nick Hornby, How to Be Good

text: I haven't had that one great love, which is good. I don't want that to be in the past, I want it to be in the future.
author: Taylor Swift

text: Child, it's a very bad thing for a woman to face the worst that can happen to her, because after she's faced the worst she can't ever really fear anything again. ...Scarlett, always save something to fear— even as you save something to love...
author: Margaret Mitchell, Gone with the Wind

text: She wondered that hope was so much harder then despair.
author: Patricia Briggs, Cry Wolf

text: Can a person steal happiness? Or is just another internal, infernal human trick?
author: Markus Zusak, The Book Thief

text: What we achieve inwardly will change outer reality.
author: Plutarch

text: We fear men so much, because we fear God so little. One fear cures another. When man's terror scares you, turn your thoughts to the wrath of God.
author: G.K. Chesterton

text: Health is the greatest possession. Contentment is the greatest treasure. Confidence is the greatest friend. Non-being is the greatest joy.
author: Lao Tzu

text: I tremble for my country when I hear of confidence expressed in me. I know too well my weakness, that our only hope is in God.
author: Robert E. Lee

text: The harmonica has musical wind, and is the breath of soul. It’s like a sad, lonely I love you lost in the breeze.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: I'm not a body with a soul, I'm a soul that has a visible part called the body.
author: Paulo Coelho, Eleven Minutes

text: That’s love: Two lonely persons keep each other safe and touch each other and talk to each other.
author: Rainer Maria Rilke

text: Life is painful. It has thorns, like the stem of a rose. Culture and art are the roses that bloom on the stem. The flower is yourself, your humanity. Art is the liberation of the humanity inside yourself.
author: Daisaku Ikeda

text: To conquer frustration, one must remain intensely focused on the outcome, not the obstacles.
author: T.F. Hodge, From Within I Rise: Spiritual Triumph Over Death and Conscious Encounters with "The Divine Presence"

text: The energy of the mind is the essence of life.
author: Aristotle, The Philosophy of Aristotle

text: Without libraries what have we? We have no past and no future.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: Happiness does not come from without, it comes from within
author: Helen Keller

text: But no matter what love throws at you, you have to believe in it. You have ot believe in love stories and prince charmings and happily ever after. That's why I write these songs. Because I think love is FEARLESS.
author: Taylor Swift

text: Anxiety's like a rocking chair. It gives you something to do, but it doesn't get you very far.
author: Jodi Picoult, Sing You Home

text: ...nothing wonderful lasted forever. Joy was as fleeting as a shooting star that crossed the evening sky, ready to blink out at any moment.
author: Nicholas Sparks, Safe Haven

text: I’m not courageous. In fact, when I shadow box I wear boxing gloves that are outfitted with flashlights.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: The moment a little boy is concerned with which is a jay and which is a sparrow, he can no longer see the birds or hear them sing.
author: Eric Berne

text: Why is the measure of love loss?
author: Jeanette Winterson, Why Be Happy When You Could Be Normal?

text: What difference do it make if the thing you scared of is real or not?
author: Toni Morrison

text: Sometimes, the only soul that can mend a broken heart is the one that broke it. For they are the ones holding all the pieces.
author: Patti Roberts, The Angels Are Here

text: Once the soul awakens, the search begins and you can never go back. From then on, you are inflamed with a special longing that will never again let you linger in the lowlands of complacency and partial fulfillment. The eternal makes you urgent. You are loath to let compromise or the threat of danger hold you back from striving toward the summit of fulfillment.
author: John O'Donohue, Anam Cara: A Book of Celtic Wisdom

text: I'm wishing he could see that music lives. Forever. That it's stronger than death. Stronger than time. And that its strength holds you together when nothing else can.
author: Jennifer Donnelly, Revolution

text: Fear is inevitable, I have to accept that, but I cannot allow it to paralyze me.
author: Isabel Allende, The Sum of Our Days: A Memoir

text: We promise according to our hopes and perform according to our fears.
author: François de La Rochefoucauld

text: I am afraid. Not of life, or death, or nothingness, but of wasting it as if I had never been.
author: Daniel Keyes, Flowers for Algernon

text: You must be the best judge of your own happiness.
author: Jane Austen, Emma

text: Some people are so much sunshine to the square inch.
author: Walt Whitman

text: you might want to decide fast. We live in a dangerous world. If you see a chance to be happy, you have to fight for it, so later you have no regrets.
author: Ilona Andrews, Magic Bleeds

text: One need not be a chamber to be haunted.
author: Emily Dickinson, The Complete Poems

text: Courage does not always roar. Sometimes courage is the quiet voice at the end of the day saying, 'I will try again tomorrow.
author: Mary Anne Radmacher

text: My soul doth magnify the Lord,And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.
author: Mary (mother of Jesus), Holy Bible: King James Version

text: When they say the sky's the limit to me that's really true
author: Michael Jackson

text: One is happy as a result of one's own efforts once one knows the necessary ingredients of happiness: simple tastes, a certain degree of courage, self denial to a point, love of work, and above all, a clear conscience.
author: George Sand

text: Fear is a very explosive emotion, but it has a short life span. It's the sprint. The marathon is hope.
author: Mike Huckabee

text: The mind can go in a thousand directions, but on this beautiful path, I walk in peace. With each step, the wind blows. With each step, a flower blooms.
author: Hanh Nhat Thich

text: There is one friend in the life of each of us who seems not a separate person, however dear and beloved, but an expansion, an interpretation, of one's self, the very meaning of one's soul.
author: Edith Wharton

text: Never give up on someone with a mental illness. When "I"is replaced by "We", illness becomes wellness.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: Even a fool recognizes that there is great sadness in a bucket of tears. But only a wise man thinks to conserve water and use that bucket to wash his car.
author: Jarod Kintz, Great Listener Seeks Mute Women

text: You couldn't erase the past. You couldn't even change it. But sometimes life offered you the opportunity to put it right.
author: Ann Brashares, Girls In Pants: The Third Summer of the Sisterhood

text: Everybody in the world is seeking happiness—and there is one sure way to find it. That is by controlling your thoughts. Happiness doesn't depend on outward conditions. It depends on inner conditions.
author: Dale Carnegie, How to Win Friends and Influence People

text: The absence of the will to live is, alas, not sufficient to make one want to die.
author: Michel Houellebecq

text: I have lived with several Zen masters -- all of them cats.
author: Eckhart Tolle, The Power of Now: A Guide to Spiritual Enlightenment

text: Do not abandon yourselves to despair. We are the Easter people and hallelujah is our song.
author: Pope John Paul II

text: Head up, heart open. To better days!
author: T.F. Hodge, From Within I Rise: Spiritual Triumph Over Death and Conscious Encounters with "The Divine Presence"

text: The man once wrote: Do not meddle in the affairs of wizards, for they are subtle and quick to anger. Tolkien had that one mostly right.I stepped forward, let the door bang closed, and snarled, "Fuck subtle.
author: Jim Butcher, Changes

text: Enlightenment is man's release from his self-incurred tutelage. Tutelage is man's inability to make use of his understanding without direction from another. Self-incurred is this tutelage when its cause lies not in lack of reason but in lack of resolution and courage to use it without direction from another. Sapere aude! 'Have courage to use your own reason!'- that is the motto of enlightenment.
author: Immanuel Kant, An Answer to the Question: What Is Enlightenment?

text: You can give without loving, but you cannot love without giving.
author: Amy Carmichael

text: Conquer the angry one by not getting angry; conquer the wicked by goodness; conquer the stingy by generosity, and the liar by speaking the truth.[Verse 223]
author: Gautama Buddha, The Dhammapada

text: Beauty is the illumination of your soul.
author: John O'Donohue, Anam Cara: A Book of Celtic Wisdom

text: We all long for something. Midgets long to be long, but I long to belong.
author: Jarod Kintz, Sleepwalking is restercise

text: Dig deep. Find your way to your soul.
author: Kami Garcia, Beautiful Creatures

text: Make voyages. Attempt them. There's nothing else.
author: Tennessee Williams, Camino Real

text: Taking a break. Been working solid for the last few hours, as opposed to working liquid, which is more drinkable. Can I pour you a glass of productivity?
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Cherish your visions and your dreams as they are the children of your soul, the blueprints of your ultimate achievements.
author: Napoleon Hill

text: Books, which we mistake for consolation, only add depth to our sorrow.
author: Orhan Pamuk, My Name is Red

text: A lonely day is God's way of saying that he wants to spend some quality time with you.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: It’s impossible,” he snapped. “Why?” “Because I’m Sebastian, Lord St. Vincent. I can’t be celibate. Everyone knows that.
author: Lisa Kleypas, Devil in Winter

text: I mean, I have the feeling that something in my mind is poisoning everything else.
author: Vladimir Nabokov
