text: Self-consciousness is the enemy of all art, be it acting, writing, painting, or living itself, which is the greatest art of all.
author: Ray Bradbury

text: I feel like a part of my soul has loved you since the beginning of everything.Maybe we’re from the same star.
author: Emery Allen

text: I am proud to represent these men and women who empower people in developing nations and promote the Peace Corps mission of peace and friendship. These volunteers are making major strides to improve the lives of people and communities around the world.
author: Kenny Marchant

text: I haven't experienced love at first sight yet. I've seen very, very beautiful girls and been awestruck but never love at first sight. I think confidence goes such a long way with women. A girl who is confident with who she is and she can really flaunt that is really sexy.
author: Tristan Wilds

text: To see what is in front of one’s nose needs a constant struggle.
author: George Orwell

text: 'Being green' is commendable, but I hope that people don't take too much pride and self-adoration because they shut off the water when they brushed their teeth. The truth of the matter is, conservation alone will do little to save our planet.
author: Naveen Jain

text: Let us not seek to satisfy our thirst for freedom by drinking from the cup of bitterness and hatred.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: In this sad world of ours sorrow comes to all and it often comes with bitter agony. Perfect relief is not possible except with time. You cannot now believe that you will ever feel better. But this is not true. You are sure to be happy again. Knowing this, truly believing it will make you less miserable now. I have had enough experience to make this statement.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: I had a dream about you. We went for a walk, though we didn’t hold hands because we went in separate directions. As I walked alone I thought about how you had the funniest way of showing affection to me.
author: Jarod Kintz, Dreaming is for lovers

text: I know I was writing stories when I was five. I don't know what I did before that. Just loafed, I suppose.
author: P.G. Wodehouse

text: In this whole screwed-up town, you're the only thing that's always been right to me,"he whispered. "I love you, Claire."She saw something that might have been just a flash of panic go across his expression, but then he steadied again. "I can't believe I'm saying this, but I do. I love you.
author: Rachel Caine, Lord of Misrule

text: The world is a sad place today. I miss the good ol’ days. Gosh, why can’t I just go back to last weekend?
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Most people believe the mind to be a mirror, more or less accurately reflecting the world outside them, not realizing on the contrary that the mind is itself the principal element of creation.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: If a woman sleeps alone it puts a shame on all men. God has a very big heart, but there is one sin He will not forgive. If a woman calls a man to her bed and he will not go.
author: Nikos Kazantzakis, Zorba the Greek

text: If you never tell anyone the truth about yourself, eventually you start to forget. The love, the heartbreak, the joy, the despair, the things I did that were good, the things I did that were shameful--if I kept them all inside, my memories of them would start to disappear. And then I would disappear.
author: Cassandra Clare, City of Heavenly Fire

text: When the clock reads 3:00, I don’t call it three o’clock, I call it three hundred, and I remember the Spartans. At 3:01, however, I remember what I was doing at 2:59, and I get back to it.
author: Jarod Kintz, The Titanic would never have sunk if it were made out of a sink.

text: ‎"What does it mean if i'm afraid? Does it mean something bad is going to happen?""No, it doesn't mean something bad is going to happen. It just means that you have the chance to be brave.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: I was an adventurer, but she was not an adventuress. She was a 'wanderess.' Thus, she didn’t care about money, only experiences - whether they came from wealth or from poverty, it was all the same to her.
author: Roman Payne, The Wanderess

text: The times you lived through, the people you shared those times with — nothing brings it all to life like an old mix tape. It does a better job of storing up memories than actual brain tissue can do. Every mix tape tells a story. Put them together, and they can add up to the story of a life.
author: Rob Sheffield, Love Is a Mix Tape

text: To fall in love is easy, even to remain in it is not difficult; our human loneliness is cause enough. But it is a hard quest worth making to find a comrade through whose steady presence one becomes steadily the person one desires to be.
author: Anna Louise Strong

text: Be sure to lie to your kids about the benevolent, all-seeing Santa Claus. It will prepare them for an adulthood of believing in God.
author: Scott Dikkers, You Are Worthless: Depressing Nuggets of Wisdom Sure to Ruin Your Day

text: If you cannot find peace within yourself, you will never find it anywhere else.
author: Marvin Gaye

text: Depression is the flaw in love. To be creatures who love, we must be creatures who can despair at what we lose, and depression is the mechanism of that despair.
author: Andrew Solomon, The Noonday Demon: An Atlas of Depression

text: But when ye come, and all the flowers are dying,If I am dead, as dead I well may be,You'll come and find the place where I am lying,And kneel and say Ave there for me,And I shall hear, though soft you tread above me,And all my grave will warmer, sweeter be,For you will bend and tell me that you love me,And I shall sleep in peace until you come to me
author: Frederick Weatherly

text: Peanut butter and jelly is like the older, more serious brother to egg salad sandwich. I’m an only child, so I eat both with incredible sadness.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: It is the power of the mind to be unconquerable.
author: Seneca, The Stoic Philosophy of Seneca: Essays and Letters

text: Those who dream by day are cognizant of many things which escape those who dream only by night. In their gray visions they obtain glimpses of eternity, and thrill, in waking, to find that they have been upon the verge of the great secret. In snatches, they learn something of the wisdom which is of good, and more of the mere knowledge which is of evil.
author: Edgar Allan Poe, Complete Tales & Poems

text: since the thing perhaps isto eat flowers and not to be afraid
author: E.E. Cummings, Complete Poems, 1904-1962

text: Thinking: the talking of the soul with itself.
author: Plato

text: Puritanism: The haunting fear that someone, somewhere, may be happy.
author: H.L. Mencken, A Mencken Chrestomathy

text: I believe with all my heart that the cliches are true, that we are our own best friends and best company, and that if you're not right for yourself, it's impossible to be right for anyone.
author: Rachel Machacek, The Science of Single: One Woman's Grand Experiment in Modern Dating, Creating Chemistry, and Finding L ove

text: The most confused you will ever get is when you try to convince your heart and spirit of something your mind knows is a lie.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: I feel sorry for anyone who is in a place where he feels strange and stupid.
author: Lois Lowry, The Giver

text: The body grows slowly and steadily but the soul grows by leaps and bounds. It may come to its full stature in an hour.
author: L.M. Montgomery, Rilla of Ingleside

text: The fiercest anger of all, the most incurable,Is that which rages in the place of dearest love.
author: Euripides, Medea and Other Plays

text: Two butterflies in two socks could walk faster than I can run. A love song will jog your memory like I jog like Roger Bannister in a wheelchair.
author: Jarod Kintz, Love quotes for the ages. Specifically ages 18-81.

text: Envy, after all, comes from wanting something that isn't yours. But grief comes from losing something you've already had.
author: Jodi Picoult, Perfect Match

text: I can't. You can't rewrite the past.
author: Jay Asher, Thirteen Reasons Why

text: It is important to feel the anger without judging it, without attempting to find meaning in it. It may take many forms: anger at the health-care system, at life, at your loved one for leaving. Life is unfair. Death is unfair. Anger is a natural reaction to the unfairness of loss.
author: Elisabeth Kubler-Ross

text: Letting go is the lesson. Letting go is always the lesson. Have you ever noticed how much of our agony is all tied up with craving and loss?
author: Susan Gordon Lydon, The Knitting Sutra: Craft as a Spiritual Practice

text: You are a little soul carrying about a corpse, as Epictetus used to say.
author: Marcus Aurelius, Meditations

text: See, as much as you want to hold on to the bitter sore memory that someone has left this world, you are still in it. And the very act of living is a tide: at first it seems to make no difference at all, and then one day you look down and see how much pain has eroded.
author: Jodi Picoult, My Sister's Keeper

text: It’s not: I jumped in, and it was cold. No. It was cold, and I jumped in. Always arrange a sentence so you appear to be fearless, when in fact you are far less than fearless—you are clueless.
author: Jarod Kintz, At even one penny, this book would be overpriced. In fact, free is too expensive, because you'd still waste time by reading it.

text: Whatever is going to happen will happen, whether we worry or not.
author: Ana Monnar

text: Understanding the difference between healthy striving and perfectionism is critical to laying down the shield and picking up your life. Research shows that perfectionism hampers success. In fact, it's often the path to depression, anxiety, addiction, and life paralysis.
author: Brené Brown, The Gifts of Imperfection: Let Go of Who You Think You're Supposed to Be and Embrace Who You Are

text: Persistence. Perfection. Patience. Power. Prioritize your passion. It keeps you sane.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: I do my best thinking at night when everyone else is sleeping. No interruptions. No noise. I like the feeling of being awake when no one else is.
author: Jennifer Niven, All the Bright Places

text: The problem, of course, was that [he] saw the world in black and white. And he got to decide what was black and what was white. You can't love a person who lives that way without fearing him too. Maybe even hating him a little.
author: Khaled Hosseini, The Kite Runner

text: Sorrow looks back, Worry looks around, Faith looks up
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Listen to the inner light; it will guide you. Listen to inner peace; it will feed you. Listen to inner love; it will transform you, it will divinise you, it will immortalise you.
author: Sri Chinmoy

text: Love is like 9.75 plus .25. That’s 10, for all of you people trying to add fractions on your fingers. I can only count using eight, because I’m too busy using my middle fingers to tell the politicians how much I love them.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Sometimes loneliness makes the loudest noise.
author: Aaron Ben-Ze'ev

text: how sad and bad and mad it was - but then, how it was sweet
author: Robert Browning

text: Don't panic. Are you sitting? You probably don't need to sit. Well, possibly. At least lean on something.
author: Maggie Stiefvater, The Raven Boys

text: We all have an old knot in the heart we wish to untie.
author: Michael Ondaatje, The Cat's Table

text: If you didn't remember something happening, was it because it never had happened? Or because you wished it hadn't?
author: Jodi Picoult, Plain Truth

text: A man's mind may be likened to a garden, which may be intelligently cultivated or allowed to run wild; but whether cultivated or neglected, it must, and will, bring forth. If no useful seeds are put into it, then an abundance of useless weed seeds will fall therein, and will continue to produce their kind.
author: James Allen, As a Man Thinketh

text: To know a man's library is, in some measure, to know a man's mind.
author: Geraldine Brooks, March

text: I keep finding myself stifled by the company of others and then crippled by loneliness when I leave them. I am terrified and I don't even know of what, because I have lost everything already.
author: Veronica Roth, Allegiant

text: Most men fear getting laughed at or humiliated by a romantic prospect while most women fear rape and death.
author: Gavin de Becker, The Gift of Fear: Survival Signals That Protect Us from Violence

text: I'd run my whole life long to reach you; paddle my way across Atlantic and Pacific; traverse Jungle and Desert to find you; climb cliffs and drop from the sky to rescue you. Anything to be close to you. Any way to say I love you.
author: Heather Kris Thomas, A Place for You and Me

text: One of the pitfalls of childhood is that one doesn't have to understand something to feel it. By the time the mind is able to comprehend what has happened, the wounds of the heart are already too deep.
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón, The Shadow of the Wind

text: YOU FEAR TO DIE?"It's not that I don't want... I mean, I've always...it's just that life is a habit that's hard to break...
author: Terry Pratchett, Reaper Man

text: And I got out of there without punching anyone, kicking anyone, or breaking down in tears. Some days the small victories are all you achieve.
author: Molly Ringle, Relatively Honest

text: It’s not that we have to quit this life one day, it’s how many things we have to quit all at once: holding hands, hotel rooms, music, the physics of falling leaves, vanilla and jasmine, poppies, smiling, anthills, the color of the sky, coffee and cashmere, literature, sparks and subway trains... If only one could leave this life slowly!
author: Roman Payne, Hope and Despair

text: She was the kind of girlfriend God gives you young, so you'll know loss the rest of your life.
author: Junot Díaz, The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao

text: He stood at the window of the empty cafe and watched the activites in the square and he said that it was good that God kept the truths of life from the young as they were starting out or else they'd have no heart to start at all.
author: Cormac McCarthy, All the Pretty Horses

text: You’re disoriented. You just woke up. You’re in the future. You’ve been asleep for eight hours.
author: Jarod Kintz, $3.33

text: In omnibus requiem quaesivi, et nusquam inveni nisi in angulo cum libro.(Everywhere I have sought peace and not found it, except in a corner with a book.)
author: Thomas à Kempis

text: Play is the highest form of research.
author: Albert Einstein

text: But you cant shut everyone out. I mean you have to have someone to love. . .someone to hold on to. . . someone--
author: Hubert Selby Jr., Requiem for a Dream

text: If loneliness was a choice, what was the other option? To settle for second-best and try to be happy with that? And was that fair to the person you settled for?
author: Lisa Kleypas, Sugar Daddy

text: A tattoo is a true poetic creation, and is always more than meets the eye. As a tattoo is grounded on living skin, so its essence emotes a poignancy unique to the mortal human condition.
author: V. Vale, Modern Primitives: An Investigation of Contemporary Adornment and Ritual

text: Sit down before fact as a little child, be prepared to give up every preconceived notion, follow humbly wherever and to whatever abysses nature leads, or you shall learn nothing. I have only begun to learn content and peace of mind since I have resolved at all risks to do this.
author: Thomas Henry Huxley, Life and Letters of Thomas Henry Huxley - Volume 1

text: To regret one’s own experiences is to arrest one’s own development. To deny one’s own experiences is to put a lie into the lips of one’s own life. It is no less than a denial of the soul.
author: Oscar Wilde, De Profundis

text: I had a dream about you. We installed Dr. Robert Jarvik’s artificial heart in a mannequin and brought it to life, only to later kill it because a creature that’s all fake heart and no brain is what’s commonly called a “politician,” and must be destroyed.
author: Dark Jar Tin Zoo, I Had a Dream About You

text: I am lonely, yet not everybody will do. I don’t know why, some people fill the gaps and others emphasize my loneliness.
author: Anaïs Nin

text: The harder you fall, the heavier your heart; the heavier your heart, the stronger you climb; the stronger you climb, the higher your pedestal.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: Happiness is part of who we are. Joy is the feeling
author: Tony DeLiso, Legacy: The Power Within

text: I had a dream about you. You were an escalator, and I was a flight of stairs. You thought I was a Luddite, and I thought I was as ostrich, because I hadn’t figured out how to put the fly in flight. One day you broke down, and then you saw that you and I weren’t so different after all.
author: Dora J. Arod, I Had a Dream About You

text: Do not let your difficulties fill you with anxiety, after all it is only in the darkest nights that stars shine more brightly.
author: Hazrat Ali Ibn Abu-Talib A.S

text: A heart has problems which mind cannot understand.
author: Santosh Kalwar, Quote Me Everyday

text: How many seconds does it take to win second? As many as it takes to win first—if you don’t use them properly.
author: Jarod Kintz, A Zebra is the Piano of the Animal Kingdom

text: Do you think it’s easy for me? No, I don’t remember you. I don’t remember holding you or talking to you or falling in love with you—but I walk around with a giant hole in my heart all the time. I feel your absence every second of the day. It aches and nothing soothes it. Losing you is bad enough, but I don’t even get the comfort of remembering that I had you once.-Haden
author: Gwen Hayes, Falling Under

text: To me, Green Arrow in the past, what people loved about Oliver Queen pre-New 52 was his relationships with other heroes. Like his friendship with Green Lantern, his animosity with Hawkman, his romance with Black Canary - these are all the things that sort of defined him.
author: Jeff Lemire

text: When I lost my sight, Werner, people said I was brave. When my father left, people said I was brave. But it is not bravery; I have no choice. I wake up and live my life. Don't you do the same?
author: Anthony Doerr, All the Light We Cannot See

text: No matter how bad your heart is broken, the world doesn't stop for your grief.
author: Faraaz Kazi

text: I don't judge people. It blurs out the center of my attention,my focus, myself.
author: Toba Beta, Betelgeuse Incident: Insiden Bait Al-Jauza

text: It is very hard for evil to take hold of the unconsenting soul.
author: Ursula K. Le Guin, A Wizard of Earthsea

text: When it comes to life the critical thing is whether you take things for granted or take them with gratitude.
author: Gilbert K. Chesterton

text: You attend the funeral, you bid the dead farewell. You grieve. Then you continue with your life. And at times the fact of her absence will hit you like a blow to the chest, and you will weep. But this will happen less and less as time goes on. She is dead. You are alive. So live.
author: Neil Gaiman, The Sandman, Vol. 6: Fables and Reflections

text: Courage and perseverance have a magical talisman, before which difficulties disappear and obstacles vanish into air.
author: John Quincy Adams

text: People who do a job that claims to be creative have to be alone to recharge their batteries. You can’t live 24 hours a day in the spotlight and remain creative. For people like me, solitude is a victory.
author: Karl Lagerfeld

text: Its more fun to think of the future than dwell on the past.
author: Sara Shepard, Unbelievable

text: I want to see beauty. In the ugly, in the sink, in the suffering, in the daily, in all the days before I die, the moments before I sleep.
author: Ann Voskamp, One Thousand Gifts: A Dare to Live Fully Right Where You Are

text: I am lost without you. I am soulless, a drifter without a home, a solitary bird in a flight to nowhere. I am all these things, and I am nothing at all. This, my darling, is my life without you. I long for you to show me how to live again.
author: Nicholas Sparks, Message in a Bottle

text: I just try to write what I think would really happen, and with grief and tragedy, there are these naturally occurring moments of levity and humor and absurdity. I think that's what life is really like. Sadness gets interrupted, and happiness gets interrupted.
author: Kaui Hart Hemmings

text: Don't waste your energy trying to change opinions ... Do your thing, and don't care if they like it.
author: Tina Fey, Bossypants

text: Sadness is but a wall between two gardens.
author: Kahlil Gibran, Sand and Foam

text: Being brave enough to be alone frees you up to invite people into your life because you want them and not because you need them.
author: Mandy Hale, The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

text: I missed you, Angel. Not one day went by that I didn't feel you missing from my life. You haunted me to the point that I began to believe Hank had gone back on his oath and killed you. I couldn't escape you and I didn't want to. You tortured me, but it was better than losing you.
author: Becca Fitzpatrick, Silence

text: Everything you need to know you have learned through your journey.
author: Paulo Coelho, The Alchemist

text: That this girl would know exactly how to shatter me.
author: Tahereh Mafi, Destroy Me

text: He made me feel unhinged . . . like he could take me apart and put me back together again and again.
author: Chelsie Shakespeare

text: The past is made out of facts... I guess the future is just hope.
author: Isaac Marion, Warm Bodies

text: Love is like a forest, I think as I kill trees by squandering toilet paper while “decorating” my ex girlfriend’s front yard.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book Has No Title

text: All problems are illusions of the mind.
author: Eckhart Tolle, Practicing the Power of Now: Essential Teachings, Meditations, and Exercises from the Power of Now

text: No man ever threw away life while it was worth keeping.
author: David Hume, Essays on Suicide and the Immortality of the Soul

text: But I can hardly sit still. I keep fidgeting, crossing one leg and then the other. I feel like I could throw off sparks, or break a window--maybe rearrange all the furniture.
author: Raymond Carver, Where I'm Calling From: New and Selected Stories

text: Rest is not idleness, and to lie sometimes on the grass under trees on a summer's day, listening to the murmur of the water, or watching the clouds float across the sky, is by no means a waste of time.
author: John Lubbock, The Use Of Life

text: Friendship! Mysterious cement of the soul, Sweet'ner of life, and solder of society.
author: Robert Blair

text: A respectable appearance is sufficient to make people more interested in your soul
author: Karl Lagerfeld

text: When a poet digs himself into a hole, he doesn't climb out. He digs deeper, enjoys the scenery, and comes out the other side enlightened.
author: Criss Jami, Venus in Arms

text: Appearance blinds, whereas words reveal.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: He who takes offense when no offense is intended is a fool, and he who takes offense when offense is intended is a greater fool.
author: Brigham Young

text: When I am king they shall not have bread and shelter only, but also teachings out of books, for a full belly is little worth where the mind is starved.
author: Mark Twain, The Prince and the Pauper

text: Do not pray for easy lives. Pray to be stronger men.[Quoting Reverend Phillips Brooks, during Remarks at Presidential Prayer Breakfast, February 7 1963]
author: John F. Kennedy

text: I had a dream about you last night. The champagne was non-alcoholic. You didn't notice, and laughed at my jokes anyway.
author: Michael Summers, I Had a Dream About You

text: Hydrogenated and androgynous milky white love is all I have to offer you. Would you like me to pour it in your coffee, or directly into your soul?
author: Jarod Kintz, The Days of Yay are Here! Wake Me Up When They're Over.

text: The strength of a love is always misjudged if we evaluate it by its immediate cause and not the stress that went before it, the dark and hollow space full of disappointment and loneliness that precedes all the great events in the heart's history.
author: Stefan Zweig, The Burning Secret and other stories

text: The arrogance that says analysing the relationship between reasons and causes is more important than writing a philosophy of shyness or sadness or friendship drives me nuts. I can't accept that.
author: Alain de Botton

text: Happiness depends on being free, and freedom depends on being courageous.
author: Marie Rutkoski, The Winner's Curse

text: I have to ask Allah's forgiveness and not get angry, because they come to me out of love, and it's not fitting that I should turn to them in hatred.
author: Abdul Qadeer Khan

text: I hold it true, whate'er befall;I feel it when I sorrow most;'Tis better to have loved and lostThan never to have loved at all.
author: Alfred Lord Tennyson, In Memoriam

text: Memory can make a thing seem to have been much more than it was.
author: Marilynne Robinson, Gilead

text: I'm fed up to the ears with old men dreaming up wars for young men to die in.
author: George S. McGovern

text: I love you, I thought. But I didn’t say it. It was not that I feared she would laugh in my face. She was far too kind for that. My fear was a greater one— that she won’t say it back.
author: Alex Flinn, Beastly

text: For the happy man prayer is only a jumble of words, until the day when sorrow comes to explain to him the sublime language by means of which he speaks to God.
author: Alexandre Dumas, The Count of Monte Cristo

text: I'd kill for a Nobel Peace Prize.
author: Steven Wright

text: I love having a ceiling fan, although sometimes I wish he wouldn't cheer so loud when I'm trying to sleep.
author: Jarod Kintz, It Occurred to Me

text: There are all kinds of ways for a relationship to be tested, even broken, some, irrevocably; it’s the endings we’re unprepared for.
author: Katherine Owen, Not To Us

text: How many people long for that "past, simpler, and better world,"I wonder, without ever recognizing the truth that perhaps it was they who were simpler and better, and not the world about them?
author: R.A. Salvatore, Streams of Silver

text: You were born a child of light’s wonderful secret— you return to the beauty you have always been.
author: Aberjhani, Visions of a Skylark Dressed in Black

text: Mathematics expresses values that reflect the cosmos, including orderliness, balance, harmony, logic, and abstract beauty.
author: Deepak Chopra

text: If you fear rejection, you won’t embrace acceptance. But don’t push yourself onto others, because they won’t pull you close.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: In these times I don't, in a manner of speaking, know what I want; perhaps I don't want what I know and want what I don't know.
author: Marsilio Ficino, The Letters of Marsilio Ficino, Vol. 3

text: My greatest desire is that the hope that has overcome fear in my country will help vanquish it around the world.
author: Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva

text: You are always free to change your mind and choose a different future, or a different past.
author: Richard Bach

text: When we are most alone is when we embrace another's loneliness.
author: Mitch Albom, The Time Keeper

text: Time is such a waste of time to think about, because the longer you ponder it, the more of it you lose. And before you know it, you don’t know it, because you are nothing but dusty worm food.
author: Jarod Kintz, I Should Have Renamed This

text: The reality of loving God is loving him like he's a Superhero who actually saved you from stuff rather than a Santa Claus who merely gave you some stuff.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: Life is like a game of chess.To win you have to make a move.Knowing which move to make comes with IN-SIGHTand knowledge, and by learning the lessons that areacculated along the way.We become each and every piece within the game called life!
author: Allan Rufus, The Master's Sacred Knowledge

text: Some men have actually died from laughing. That's so funny I fear for my own life.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: If someone loves you, they should not be envious of you pouring your heart and soul and time and energy into the things that you are passionate about, but instead....they should love you MORE because you are so involved in those things.
author: Sharon Swan

text: I just don't see why the past has to matter.
author: Cassandra Clare, City of Fallen Angels

text: The probability that we may fail in the struggle ought not to deter usfrom the support of a cause we believe to be just.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: People Die...Beauty Fades...Love Changes...And You Will Always Be Alone
author: L.J. Smith, Night World, No. 3

text: Don't forget, a person's greatest emotional need is to feel appreciated.
author: H. Jackson Brown Jr., Life's Little Instruction Book: 511 Suggestions, Observations, and Reminders on How to Live a Happy and Rewarding Life

text: You can only be afraid of what you think you know.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: That's what makes it so right. Your eyes—your soul is there, but the rest of you is still so undefined. That's the beauty of childhood. The eyes show everything you've seen so far, but the rest of you is still so open to possibility, to whatever you might become.
author: Bree Despain, The Dark Divine

text: Risk means 'shit happens' or 'good luck
author: Toba Beta, Betelgeuse Incident: Insiden Bait Al-Jauza

text: Don’t let mental blocks control you. Set yourself free. Confront your fear and turn the mental blocks into building blocks.
author: Roopleen, Words to inspire the winner in YOU

text: It frightens me, the awful truth, of how sweet life can be...
author: Bob Dylan

text: My parents are going to kill me!""That seems rather harsh...
author: Garth Nix, Sir Thursday

text: Every day I think today could be my last day. My only fear is dying without telling her I love her one more time. All I pray for is one more time.
author: Jarod Kintz, This Book is Not FOR SALE

text: Peace does not mean an absence of conflicts; differences will always be there. Peace means solving these differences through peaceful means; through dialogue, education, knowledge; and through humane ways.
author: Dalai Lama XIV

text: To understand the immeasurable, the mind must be extraordinarily quiet, still.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: Knowing how to be solitary is central to the art of loving. When we can be alone, we can be with others without using them as a means of escape.
author: bell hooks

text: Peace, commerce and honest friendship with all nations; entangling alliances with none.
author: Thomas Jefferson

text: How it is that animals understand things I do not know, but it is certain that they do understand. Perhaps there is a language which is not made of words and everything in the world understands it. Perhaps there is a soul hidden in everything and it can always speak, without even making a sound, to another soul.
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett, A Little Princess

text: So the unwanting soulsees what's hidden,and the ever-wanting soulsees only what it wants.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Very few beings really seek knowledge in this world. Mortal or immortal, few really ask. On the contrary, they try to wring from the unknown the answers they have already shaped in their own minds -- justifications, confirmations, forms of consolation without which they can't go on. To really ask is to open the door to the whirlwind. The answer may annihilate the question and the questioner.
author: Anne Rice, The Vampire Lestat

text: People who lack the clarity, courage, or determination to follow their own dreams will often find ways to discourage yours. Live your truth and don't EVER stop!
author: Steve Maraboli, Life, the Truth, and Being Free

text: Can you look without the voice in your head commenting, drawing conclusions, comparing, or trying to figure something out?
author: Eckhart Tolle, A New Earth: Awakening to Your Life's Purpose

text: There is no such thing as inner peace. There is only nervousness and death.
author: Fran Lebowitz

text: ... millions long for immortality who don't know what to do with themselves on a rainy Sunday afternoon.
author: Susan Ertz

text: We all know that the great memories of our childhood are the little triumphs - it doesn't really matter whether that was in writing, art, on the hockey field or on the football field. It's something that makes you feel - 'I can do this stuff.'
author: Michael Morpurgo

text: I am so fresh in soul and spirit that life gushes and bubbles around me in a thousand springs.
author: Robert Schumann

text: I will be stronger than my sadness.
author: Jasmine Warga, My Heart and Other Black Holes

text: You are mistaken, Mr. Darcy, if you suppose that the mode of your declaration affected me in any other way, than as it spared the concern which I might have felt in refusing you, had you behaved in a more gentlemanlike manner."(Elizabeth Bennett)
author: Jane Austen, Pride and Prejudice

text: The mind of man is capable of anything.
author: Joseph Conrad, Heart of Darkness

text: Where to look if you've lost your mind?
author: Bernard Malamud, The Fixer

text: Happy is the man who has broken the chains which hurt the mind, and has given up worrying once and for all.”"Be patient and tough; one day this pain will be useful to you.
author: Ovid, Metamorphoses

text: Single” is an opportunity to live life on your own terms and not apologize.
author: Mandy Hale, The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

text: What you are to do without me I cannot imagine.
author: George Bernard Shaw, Pygmalion

text: My most important piece of advice to all you would-be writers: When you write, try to leave out all the parts readers skip.
author: Elmore Leonard, Elmore Leonard's 10 Rules of Writing

text: Instead of focusing on how much you can accomplish, focus on how much you can absolutely love what you’re doing.
author: Leo Babauta

text: Thank you for life, and all the little ups and downs that make it worth living.
author: Travis Barker

text: You have everything you need for complete peace and total happiness right now.
author: Wayne W. Dyer

text: ‎Hold yourself responsible for a higher standard than anybody else expects of you. Never excuse yourself. Never pity yourself. Be a hard master to yourself-and be lenient to everybody else.
author: Henry Ward Beecher

text: I have such a fear of finding another like myself, and such a desire to find one! I am so utterly lonely, but I also have such a fear that my isolation be broken through, and I no longer be the head and ruler of my universe.
author: Anaïs Nin, House of Incest

text: You said move on, where do I go?
author: Katy Perry

text: I'll sleep forever and meet you in my dreams; it'll hurt less anyway. But the pain of losing you was worth the pleasure of having you, and I rest in bed knowing you'll know for the rest of your life what you are missing.-Karen Quan and Jarod Kintz
author: Karen Quan, liQUID PROse QUOtes

text: I have always felt that doubt was the beginning of wisdom, and the fear of God was the end of wisdom.
author: Clarence Darrow, The Story of My Life

text: It’s OKAY to be scared. Being scared means you’re about to do something really, really brave.
author: Mandy Hale, The Single Woman: Life, Love, and a Dash of Sass

text: There is no such thing as a good influence. Because to influence a person is to give him one's own soul. He does not think his natural thoughts, or burn with his natural passions. His virtures are not real to him. His sins, if there are such thing as sins, are borrowed. He becomes an echo of someone else's music, an actor of a part that has not been written for him.
author: Oscar Wilde, The Picture of Dorian Gray

text: Maybe sometimes we don't do the right thing because the wrong thing looks more dangerous, and we don't want to look scared, so we go and do the wrong thing just because it's dangerous. We're more concerned with not looking scared than with judging right.
author: Philip Pullman, The Amber Spyglass

text: When all is said and done, the weather and love are the two elements about which one can never be sure.
author: Alice Hoffman, Here on Earth

text: But I have my life, I’m living it. It’s twisted, exhausting, uncertain, and full of guilt, but nonetheless, there’s something there.
author: Banana Yoshimoto, The Lake

text: Everyone, either from modesty or egotism, hides away the best and most delicate of his soul’s possessions; to gain the esteem of others, we must only ever show our ugliest sides; this is how we keep ourselves on the common level
author: Gustave Flaubert, November

text: To be alone is to be different, to be different is to be alone.
author: Suzanne Gordon

text: I'll miss looking through the forest–your green and hazel eyes. I love the way they shimmered and changed shade with your mood, and how I could see the desire in your emerald gaze.-Karen Quan and Jarod Kintz
author: Karen Quan, liQUID PROse QUOtes

text: What the soul hardly realizes is that, unbeliever or not, his loneliness is really a homesickness for God.
author: Dom Hubert Van Zeller

text: The first peace, which is the most important, is that which comes within the souls of people when they realize their relationship, their oneness with the universe and all its powers, and when they realize at the center of the universe dwells the Great Spirit, and that its center is really everywhere, it is within each of us.
author: Black Elk

text: We would rather be ruined than changedWe would rather die in our dreadThan climb the cross of the momentAnd let our illusions die.
author: W.H. Auden, The Age of Anxiety: A Baroque Eclogue

text: I am fallen, flawed and imperfect. Yet drenched in the grace and mercy that is found in Jesus Christ, there is strength
author: Adam Young

text: The Portuguese call it saudade: a longing for something so indefinite as to be indefinable. Love affairs, miseries of life, the way things were, people already dead, those who left and the ocean that tossed them on the shores of a different land — all things born of the soul that can only be felt.
author: Anthony De Sa, Barnacle Love

text: The past can teach us, nurture us, but it cannot sustain us. The essence of life is change, and we must move ever forward or the soul will wither and die.
author: Susanna Kearsley, Mariana

text: When we long for life without difficulties, remind us that oaks grow strong in contrary winds and diamonds are made under pressure.
author: Peter Marshall

text: We should ask God to increase our hope when it is small, awaken it when it is dormant, confirm it when it is wavering, strengthen it when it is weak, and raise it up when it is overthrown.
author: John Calvin

text: La tristesse durera toujours.[The sadness will last forever.]
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: Wisdom comes through suffering.Trouble, with its memories of pain,Drips in our hearts as we try to sleep,So men against their willLearn to practice moderation.Favours come to us from gods.
author: Aeschylus, Agamemnon

text: Soulmate"is an overused term, but a true soul connection is very rare, and very real.
author: Hilary Duff, Devoted

text: When we were we, with you is the only place I wanted to be. When we were just two separate souls, I used to dream of a place where we'd come together as one.-Jarod Kintz and Karen Quan
author: Jarod Kintz, liQUID PROse QUOtes

text: Morality is temporary, wisdom is permanent.
author: Hunter S. Thompson, Kingdom of Fear: Loathsome Secrets of a Star-Crossed Child in the Final Days of the American Century

text: If he can't handle you at your worst then he does not deserve you at your best. Real love means seeing beyond the words spoken out of pain, and instead seeing a person's soul.
author: Shannon L. Alder, 300 Questions LDS Couples Should Ask Before Marriage

text: Never worry alone. When anxiety grabs my mind, it is self-perpetuating. Worrisome thoughts reproduce faster than rabbits, so one of the most powerful ways to stop the spiral of worry is simply to disclose my worry to a friend... The simple act of reassurance from another human being [becomes] a tool of the Spirit to cast out fear -- because peace and fear are both contagious.
author: John Ortberg Jr., The Me I Want to Be: Becoming God's Best Version of You

text: Two turtle doves will show theeWhere my cold ashes lieAnd sadly murmuring tell theeHow in tears I did die
author: Nikolai Gogol

text: It’s odd, isn’t it? People die every day and the world goes on like nothing happened. But when it’s a person you love, you think everyone should stop and take notice. That they ought to cry and light candles and tell you that you’re not alone.
author: Kristina McMorris, Letters From Home

text: A man always finds it hard to realize that he may have finally lost a woman's love, however badly he may have treated her.
author: Arthur Conan Doyle, The Musgrave Ritual

text: This fire that we call Loving is too strong for human minds. But just right for human souls.
author: Aberjhani, Elemental: The Power of Illuminated Love

text: When you concentrate your energy purposely on the future possibility that you aspire to realize, your energy is passed on to it and makes it attracted to you with a force stronger than the one you directed towards it.
author: Stephen Richards, Think Your way to Success: Let Your Dreams Run Free

text: Begin, be bold, and venture to be wise.
author: Horace

text: They say you fear what you don't understand. Maybe that's why every time I'm in South Florida, and I hear someone talking in Spanish, I always shit my pants.
author: Jarod Kintz, It Occurred to Me

text: There are six reasons anyone does anything: Love. Faith. Greed. Boredom. Fear..."he said, ticking them off on his fingers; but he lingered on the last, drawing a deep breath before he said, "Revenge.
author: Ally Carter, Don't Judge a Girl by Her Cover

text: You don't need another Human Being to make your life complete, but let's be honest. Having your wounds kissed by someone who doesn't see them as disasters In your soul, but cracks to put their love into, Is the most calming thing In this World.
author: Emery Allen

text: it takes as much courage to have tried and failed as it does to have tried and succeded.
author: Anne Morrow Lindbergh

text: I've got a theory, it could be bunnies...I've got a theor-Bunnies aren't just cute like everybody supposesThey've got them hoppy legs and twitchy little noses.And what's with all the carrots-?What do they need such good eyesight for anyway?Bunnies, bunnies it must be bunnies!...or maybe midgets...
author: Joss Whedon

text: Lying is easy. But it's lonely.""What do you mean?""When you lie to everyone about everything, what's left? What's true?""Nothing,"I say."Exactly.
author: Victoria Schwab, The Archived

text: Everything you want is on the other side of fear.
author: Jack Canfield

text: Whatever happens to your body, your soul will survive, untouched...
author: J.K. Rowling, Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows

text: What the mind can conceive and believe, and the heart desire, you can achieve.
author: Norman Vincent Peale

text: What keeps life fascinating is the constant creativity of the soul.
author: Deepak Chopra, Life After Death: The Burden of Proof

text: There may be a great fire in our soul, yet no one ever comes to warm himself at it, and the passers-by see only a wisp of smoke.
author: Vincent van Gogh

text: There are a few moments in your life when you are truly and completely happy, and you remember to give thanks. Even as it happens you are nostalgic for the moment, you are tucking it away in your scrapbook.
author: David Benioff, When the Nines Roll Over and Other Stories

text: You are not what others think you are. You are what God knows you are.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: You are one thing only. You are a Divine Being. An all-powerful Creator. You are a Deity in jeans and a t-shirt, and within you dwells the infinite wisdom of the ages and the sacred creative force of All that is, will be and ever was.
author: Anthon St. Maarten, Divine Living: The Essential Guide To Your True Destiny

text: What kind of life can you have in a house without books?
author: Sherman Alexie, Flight

text: The direction of your focus is the direction your life will move. Let yourself move toward what is good, valuable, strong and true.
author: Ralph Marston

text: Sometimes when you least expect it, the tables turn and that scary feeling that has taken hold of you for so long somehow turns into hope
author: David Archuleta, Chords of Strength: A Memoir of Soul, Song and the Power of Perseverance

text: I think anyone who opened their heart enough to love without restraint and subsequently were devastated by loss knows that in that moment you are forever changed; a apart of you is no longer whole. Some will never again love with that level of abandon where life is perceived as innocent and the threat of loss seems implausible. Love and loss, therefore, are linked.
author: Donna Lynn Hope

text: Reading a book is like having the ability to dip a straw into the author’s soul and sip and slurp without lowering the water table of wisdom.
author: Jarod Kintz, The Days of Yay are Here! Wake Me Up When They're Over.

text: Beauty is the purest feeling of the soul. Beauty arises when soul is satisfied.
author: Amit Ray, Nonviolence: The Transforming Power

text: I think it would be neat to meet a man who slept with one eye open, especially if that man was a Cyclops.
author: Jarod Kintz, It Occurred to Me

text: What needs my Shakespeare for his honoured bones,The labor of an age in pilèd stones,Or that his hallowed relics should be hidUnder a star-y-pointing pyramid?Dear son of memory, great heir of fame,What need'st thou such weak witness of thy name?
author: John Milton, The Complete Poetry

text: Even in the mud and scum of things, something always, always sings.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: It is foolish to wish for beauty. Sensible people never either desire it for themselves or care about it in others. If the mind be but well cultivated, and the heart well disposed, no one ever cares for the exterior.
author: Anne Brontë, Agnes Grey

text: Pity me that the heart is slow to learnWhat the swift mind beholds at every turn.
author: Edna St. Vincent Millay, The Harp-Weaver and Other Poems

text: Nostalgia in reverse, the longing for yet another strange land, grew especially strong in spring.
author: Vladimir Nabokov, Mary

text: I know that it's easier to look at death than it is to look at pain, because while death is irrevocable, and the grief will lessen in time, pain is too often merely relentless and irreversible.
author: Robert Goolrick, The End of the World as We Know It: Scenes from a Life

text: The sky is not my limit...I am.
author: T.F. Hodge, From Within I Rise: Spiritual Triumph Over Death and Conscious Encounters with "The Divine Presence"

text: The face is a picture of the mind with the eyes as its interpreter.
author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

text: All should be laid open to you without reserve, for there is not a truth existing which I fear, or would wish unknown to the whole world.
author: Thomas Jefferson, Writings: Autobiography/Notes on the State of Virginia/Public & Private Papers/Addresses/Letters

text: Action and reaction, ebb and flow, trial and error, change - this is the rhythm of living. Out of our over-confidence, fear; out of our fear, clearer vision, fresh hope. And out of hope, progress.
author: Bruce Barton

text: I wish the night would end,I wish the day'd begin,I wish it would rain or snow,or the wind would blow,or the grass would grow,I wish I had yesterday,I wish there were games to play...
author: V.C. Andrews, Flowers in the Attic

text: The only thing new in the world is the history you do not know.
author: Harry S. Truman

text: Control Your Own Destiny or Someone Else Will
author: Jack Welch

text: No name-calling truly bites deep unless, in some dark part of us, we believe it. If we are confident enough then it is just noise.
author: Laurell K. Hamilton, A Stroke of Midnight

text: I drink to separate my body from my soul.
author: Oscar Wilde

text: Boredom can be a lethal thing on a small island.
author: Christopher Moore, Island of the Sequined Love Nun

text: I am terrified by this dark thingThat sleeps in me;All day I feel its soft, feathery turnings, its malignity.
author: Sylvia Plath, Ariel

text: I began to understand that suffering and disappointments and melancholy are there not to vex us or cheapen us or deprive us of our dignity but to mature and transfigure us.
author: Hermann Hesse, Peter Camenzind

text: Whoever said that the past isn't dead had it backward. It's the future that's already dead, already played out.
author: Gayle Forman, Where She Went

text: Got no checkbooks, got no banks. Still I'd like to express my thanks - I've got the sun in the mornin' and the moon at night.
author: Irving Berlin

text: I have a realistic grasp of my own strengths and weaknesses. My mind is my weapon. My brother has his sword, King Robert has his warhammer, and I have my mind… and a mind needs books as a sword needs a whetstone, if it is to keep its edge. That’s why I read so much, Jon Snow.
author: George R.R. Martin, A Game of Thrones

text: I had a dream about you last night... you were a giant slinky and I watched you fall down the stairs.
author: Amy Summers, I Had a Dream About You

text: Give me strength, not to be better than my enemies, but to defeat my greatest enemy, the doubts within myself. Give me strength for a straight back and clear eyes, so when life fades, as the setting sun, my spirit may come to you without shame.
author: P.C. Cast

text: Quiet the mind, and the soul will speak.
author: Ma Jaya Sati Bhagavati, The 11 Karmic Spaces: Choosing Freedom from the Patterns That Bind You

text: Three years? That's a thousand tomorrows, ma'am.
author: Karen Kingsbury

text: Except our own thoughts, there is nothing absolutely in our power.
author: René Descartes

text: Ignorance is the parent of fear.
author: Herman Melville, Moby-Dick; or, The Whale

text: The freedom to fail is vital if you're going to succeed. Most successful people fail from time to time, and it is a measure of their strength that failure merely propels them into some new attempt at success.
author: Michael Korda

text: I'll never wake up in a good mood again.I'm tired of these stinky boots
author: Jim Morrison, An American Prayer

text: I have high blood sugars, and Type 2 diabetes is not going to kill me. But I just have to eat right, and exercise, and lose weight, and watch what I eat, and I will be fine for the rest of my life.
author: Tom Hanks

text: How can a person deal with anxiety? You might try what one fellow did. He worried so much that he decided to hire someone to do his worrying for him. He found a man who agreed to be his hired worrier for a salary of $200,000 per year. After the man accepted the job, his first question to his boss was, "Where are you going to get $200,000 per year?"To which the man responded, "That's your worry.
author: Max Lucado

text: Anne was always glad in the happiness of her friends; but it is sometimes a little lonely to be surrounded everywhere by happiness that is not your own.
author: L.M. Montgomery, Anne of the Island

text: I didnt pay atteniton to times or distance, instead focusing on how it felt just to be in motion, knowing it wasn't about the finish line but how I got there that mattered.
author: Sarah Dessen, The Truth About Forever

text: I spent the rest of the day in someone else's story. The rare moments that I put the book down, my own pain returned in burning stabs.
author: Amy Plum, Die for Me

text: When those you love die, the best you can do is honor their spirit for as long as you live. You make a commitment that you're going to take whatever lesson that person or animal was trying to teach you, and you make it true in your own life... it's a positive way to keep their spirit alive in the world, by keeping it alive in yourself.
author: Patrick Swayze, The Time of My Life

text: You can exist without your soul, you know, as long as your brain and heart are still working. But you’ll have no sense of self anymore, no memory, no . . . anything. There’s no chance at all of recovery. You’ll just — exist. As an empty shell. And your soul is gone forever . . . lost.
author: J.K. Rowling, Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban

text: One of the greatest tragedies in life is to lose your own sense of self and accept the version of you that is expected by everyone else.
author: K.L. Toth

text: When I step out on stage in front of thousands of people, I don't feel that I'm being brave. It can take much more courage to express true feelings to one person. [...] In spite of the risks, the courage to be honest and intimate opens the way to self-discovery. It offers what we all want, the promise of love.
author: Michael Jackson, Dancing the Dream

text: Fear can only grow in darkness. Once you face fear with light, you win.
author: Steve Maraboli, Life, the Truth, and Being Free

text: Here is a list of terrible things,The jaws of sharks, a vultures wingsThe rabid bite of the dogs of war,The voice of one who went before,But most of all the mirror's gaze,Which counts us out our numbered days.
author: Clive Barker, Days of Magic, Nights of War

text: Disappointments in love, even betrayals and losses, serve the soul at the very moment they seem in life to be tragedies. The soul is partly in time and partly in eternity. We might remember the part that resides in eternity when we feel despair over the part that is in life.
author: Thomas Moore, Care of the Soul: Guide for Cultivating Depth and Sacredness in Everyday Life

text: A man will be imprisoned in a room with a door that's unlocked and opens inwards; as long as it does not occur to him to pull rather than push.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein, Culture and Value

text: People who say that yesterday was better than today are ultimately devaluing their own existence.
author: Karl Lagerfeld

text: Meditation is a way for nourishing and blossoming the divinity within you.
author: Amit Ray, Meditation: Insights and Inspirations

text: The world is 3 days: As for yesterday, it has vanished along with all that was in it. As for tomorrow, you may never see it. As for today, it is yours, so work on it.
author: al-Hasan al-Basri

text: Undo it, take it back, make every day the previous one until I am returned to the day before the one that made you gone. Or set me on an airplane traveling west, crossing the date line again and again, losing this day, then that, until the day of loss still lies ahead, and you are here instead of sorrow.
author: Nessa Rapoport

text: Laugh, and the world laughs with you;Weep, and you weep alone;For the sad old earth must borrow its mirth,But has trouble enough of its own.
author: Ella Wheeler Wilcox, Poems of Passion

text: A great song should lift your heart, warm the soul and make you feel good.
author: Colbie Caillat

text: The great love is gone. There are still little loves - friend to friend, brother to sister, student to teacher. Will you deny yourself comfort at the hearthfire of a cottage because you may no longer sit by the fireplace of a palace? Will you deny yourself to those who reach out to you in hopes of warming themselves at your hearthfire?
author: Mercedes Lackey, Magic's Pawn

text: Ten long trips around the sun since I last saw that smile, but only joy and thankfulness that on a tiny world in the vastness, for a couple of moments in the immensity of time, we were one.
author: Ann Druyan

text: Because misery, and degradation, and death, and nothing that God or Satan could inflict would have parted us, you, of your own will did it. I have no broken your heart - you have broken it; and in breaking it, you have broken mine. So much the worse for me that I am strong.
author: Emily Brontë, Wuthering Heights

text: There's a sorrow and pain in everyone's life, but every now and then there's a ray of light that melts the loneliness in your heart and brings comfort like hot soup and a soft bed.
author: Hubert Selby Jr., Requiem for a Dream

text: You read and write and sing and experience, thinking that one day these things will build the character you admire to live as. You love and lose and bleed best you can, to the extreme, hoping that one day the world will read you like the poem you want to be.
author: Charlotte Eriksson

text: Restlessness and impatience change nothing except our peace and joy. Peace does not dwell in outward things, but in the heart prepared to wait trustfully and quietly on Him who has all things safely in His hands.
author: Elisabeth Elliot

text: Stand firm in the Lord. Stand firm and let Him fight your battle. Do not try to fight alone.
author: Francine Rivers, A Voice in the Wind

text: There are not enough days in forever to allow me to fully express the depth of my love for you.
author: Steve Maraboli, Unapologetically You: Reflections on Life and the Human Experience

text: Talk to me about the truth of religion and I'll listen gladly. Talk to me about the duty of religion and I'll listen submissively. But don't come talking to me about the consolations of religion or I shall suspect that you don't understand.
author: C.S. Lewis, A Grief Observed

text: There is peace even in the storm
author: Vincent van Gogh, The Letters of Vincent van Gogh

text: Peace is present right here and now, in ourselves and in everything we do and see. Every breath we take, every step we take, can be filled with peace, joy, and serenity. The question is whether or not we are in touch with it. We need only to be awake, alive in the present moment.
author: Hanh Nhat Thich, Peace Is Every Step: The Path of Mindfulness in Everyday Life

text: A young outcast will often feel that there is something wrong with himself, but as he gets older, grows more confident in who he is, he will adapt, he will begin to feel that there is something wrong with everyone else.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: Spirituality is not to be learned by flight from the world, or by running away from things, or by turning solitary and going apart from the world. Rather, we must learn an inner solitude wherever or with whomsoever we may be. We must learn to penetrate things and find God there.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: The simple step of a courageous individual is not to take part in the lie. "One word of truth outweighs the world.
author: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn

text: My first feeling was that there was no way to continue. Writing isn't like math;in math, two plus two always equals four no matter what your mood is like. With writing, the way you feel changes everything.
author: Stephenie Meyer, Midnight Sun

text: Spirituality is recognizing and celebrating that we are all inextricably connected to each other by a power greater than all of us, and that our connection to that power and to one another is grounded in love and compassion. Practicing spirituality brings a sense of perspective, meaning and purpose to our lives.
author: Brené Brown, The Gifts of Imperfection: Let Go of Who You Think You're Supposed to Be and Embrace Who You Are

text: when I am feelinglowall i have to do iswatch my catsand mycouragereturns
author: Charles Bukowski

text: If everyone in the world sat quietly at the same time, closed their eyes and concentrated as hard as they could on peace and goodwill, all the killing and cruelty in the world would continue. And probably increase.
author: George Carlin

text: When your back is to the wall and you are facing fear head on, the only way is forward and through it.
author: Stephen Richards, Releasing You from Fear

text: It's not our job to toughen our children up to face a cruel and heartless world. It's our job to raise children who will make the world a little less cruel and heartless.
author: L.R. Knost, Two Thousand Kisses a Day: Gentle Parenting Through the Ages and Stages

text: For those who know the value of and exquisite taste of solitary freedom (for one is only free when alone), the act of leaving is the bravest and most beautiful of all.
author: Isabelle Eberhardt, The Nomad: The Diaries of Isabelle Eberhardt

text: it always sound scarier when a hollerer talk soft.
author: Kathryn Stockett, The Help

text: Put your heart, mind, and soul into even your smallest acts. This is the secret of success.
author: Swami Sivananda

text: You get lonely, is what it is. A person's not supposed to go through life with absolutely nobody. It's not normal. The longer you go by yourself the weirder you get, and the weirder you get the longer you go by yourself. It's a loop and you gotta do something to get out of it.
author: Jim Shepard

text: I’ll read my books and I’ll drink coffee and I’ll listen to music, and I’ll bolt the door."( A Boy in France : Saturday Evening Post CCXVII, March 31, 1945)
author: J.D. Salinger

text: Seek out that particular mental attribute which makes you feel most deeply and vitally alive, along with which comes the inner voice which says, 'This is the real me,' and when you have found that attitude, follow it.
author: William James, The Principles of Psychology

text: Real courage is doing the right thing when nobody's looking. Doing the unpopular thing because it's what you believe, and the heck with everybody.
author: Justin Cronin, The Summer Guest

text: Do not be afraid. Do not be satisfied with mediocrity. Put out into the deep and let down your nets for a catch.
author: Pope John Paul II

text: We have all known the long loneliness and we have learned that the only solution is love and that love comes with community.
author: Dorothy Day, The Long Loneliness: The Autobiography of the Legendary Catholic Social Activist

text: The only way for you to keep your mind straight is to run from those who would confuse you.
author: Kristin Cashore, Graceling

text: Silent solitude makes true speech possible and personal. If I am not in touch with my own belovedness, then I cannot touch the sacredness of others. If I am estranged from myself, I am likewise a stranger to others.
author: Brennan Manning, Abba's Child: The Cry of the Heart for Intimate Belonging

text: You are evidence of your mother's strength, especially if you are a rebellious knucklehead and regardless she has always maintained her sanity.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: I don't know why it should be, I am sure; but the sight of another man asleep in bed when I am up, maddens me.
author: Jerome K. Jerome, Three Men in a Boat

text: Everyone must leave something behind when he dies . . . Something your hand touched some way so your soul has somewhere to go when you die . . . It doesn't matter what you do, so long as you change something from the way it was before you touched it into something that's like you after you take your hands away.
author: Ray Bradbury, Fahrenheit 451

text: Shock is a merciful condition. It allows you to get through disaster with a necessary distance between you and your feelings.
author: Lisa Kleypas, Sugar Daddy

text: The artist produces for the liberation of his soul. It is his nature to create as it is the nature of water to run down the hill.
author: W. Somerset Maugham

text: Live life as though nobody is watching, and express yourself as though everyone is listening.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: The rich are always going to say that, you know, just give us more money and we'll go out and spend more and then it will all trickle down to the rest of you. But that has not worked the last 10 years, and I hope the American public is catching on.
author: Warren Buffett

text: She wanted to lose herself in him. To tie his arms around her like a tourniquet.If she showed him how much she needed him, he'd run away.
author: Rainbow Rowell, Eleanor & Park

text: If anger were mileage, I'd be a very frequent flyer, right up there in First Class.
author: Gina Barreca

text: A poet should be so crafty with words that he is envied even for his pains.
author: Criss Jami, Killosophy

text: You are never alone. You are eternally connected with everyone.
author: Amit Ray, Meditation: Insights and Inspirations

text: I have lived with you and loved you, and now you are gone. Gone where I cannot follow, until I have finished all of my days.
author: Victoria Hanley, The Seer and the Sword

text: Your inner strength is your outer foundation
author: Allan Rufus

text: Everything that is really great and inspiring is created by the individual who can labor in freedom.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Me and you, we got more yesterday than anybody. We need some kind of tomorrow.
author: Toni Morrison, Beloved

text: There are times in our lives when we have to realize our past is precisely what it is, and we cannot change it. But we can change the story we tell ourselves about it, and by doing that, we can change the future.
author: Eleanor Brown, The Weird Sisters

text: I wouldn't have missed a single minute of it, Not for the whole world.
author: Stephen King

text: Who knew that the path to a womans heart was through the soul of an honest man?
author: Melissa de la Cruz

text: He who is not everyday conquering some fear has not learned the secret of life.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Flowers and fear are a lot alike. For one, flowers and fear have a distinct smell, and two, I’m currently trying to grow both in my garden.
author: Jarod Kintz, There are Two Typos of People in This World: Those Who Can Edit and Those Who Can't

text: It's so trendy, almost bleeding to death. All the cool girls are doing it.
author: Francine Pascal, Fearless

text: The ‘Muse’ is not an artistic mystery, but a mathematical equation. The gift are those ideas you think of as you drift to sleep. The giver is that one you think of when you first awake.
author: Roman Payne

text: My idea of God is not a divine idea. It has to be shattered time after time. He shatters it Himself.
author: C.S. Lewis, A Grief Observed

text: The trick. . .is to find the balance between the bright colors of humor and the serious issues of identity, self-loathing, and the possibility for intimacy and love when it seems no longer possible or, sadder yet, no longer necessary.
author: Wendy Wasserstein

text: The poor man shuddered, overflowed with an angelic joy; he declared in his transport that this would last through life; he said to himself that he really had not suffered enough to deserve such radiant happiness, and he thanked God, in the depths of his soul, for having permitted that he, a miserable man, should be so loved by this innocent being.
author: Victor Hugo, Les Misérables

text: I waited for dawn, but only because I had forgotten how hard mornings were. For a second I'd be normal. Then came the dim awareness of something off, out of place. Then the truth came crashing down and that was it for the rest of the day. Sunlight was reproof. Shouldn't I feel better than I had in the dead of night.
author: Francine Prose, Goldengrove

text: Courage is the price that life exacts for granting peace.
author: Amelia Earhart

text: Do not look for happiness outside yourself. The awakened seek happiness inside.
author: Peter Deunov

text: Success will be within your reach only when you start reaching out for it.
author: Stephen Richards, Think Your way to Success: Let Your Dreams Run Free

text: He feared me as many men fear women: because their mistresses (or their wives) understand them. They are scarcely adult, some men: they wish women to understand them, and to that end they tell them all their secrets; and then, when they are properly understood, they hate their women for understanding them.
author: Julian Barnes, Flaubert's Parrot

text: Life is all memory, except for the one present moment that goes by you so quickly you hardly catch it going.
author: Tennessee Williams

text: Sometimes I wish life was written pencil so we could erase it and write it all over again.
author: Thisuri Wanniarachchi, COLOMBO STREETS

text: A man who is intimate with God is not intimidated by man.
author: Leonard Ravenhill

text: Even as your body betrays you, your mind denies it.
author: Sara Gruen, Water for Elephants

text: To be heroic is to be courageous enough to die for something; to be inspirational is to be crazy enough to live a little.
author: Criss Jami, Venus in Arms

text: I plead with you--never, ever give up on hope, never doubt, never tire, and never become discouraged. Be not afraid.
author: Pope John Paul II, Pope John Paul II: In My Own Words

text: There is one spectacle grander than the sea, that is the sky; there is one spectacle grander than the sky, that is the interior of the soul.
author: Victor Hugo, Les Misérables

text: You must read, you must persevere, you must sit up nights, you must inquire, and exert the utmost power of your mind. If one way does not lead to the desired meaning, take another; if obstacles arise, then still another; until, if your strength holds out, you will find that clear which at first looked dark.
author: Giovanni Boccaccio

text: Literature is my Utopia. Here I am not disenfranchised. No barrier of the senses shuts me out from the sweet, gracious discourses of my book friends. They talk to me without embarrassment or awkwardness.
author: Helen Keller, The Story of My Life

text: The pen will never be able to move fast enough to write down every word discovered in the space of memory. Some things have been lost forever, other things will perhaps be remembered again, and still other things have been lost and found and lost again. There is no way to be sure of any this.
author: Paul Auster, The Invention of Solitude

text: Happiness can only be achieved by looking inward & learning to enjoy whatever life has and this requires transforming greed into gratitude.
author: John Chrysostom

text: Don’t worry if people think you’re crazy. You are crazy. You have that kind of intoxicating insanity that lets other people dream outside of the lines and become who they’re destined to be.
author: Jennifer Elisabeth, Born Ready: Unleash Your Inner Dream Girl

text: When you rise in the morning, give thanks for the light, for your life, for your strength. Give thanks for your food and for the joy of living. If you see no reason to give thanks, the fault lies in yourself.
author: Tecumseh

text: Some of us walk around with a necklace of hope, an armour of sanity, but at the end of the day, they always come off. We reveal our naked, vulnerable, real selves.
author: Karen Quan, Write like no one is reading 2

text: If money’s the god people worship, I’d rather go worship the devil instead.
author: Jess C. Scott, Rockstar

text: Language is the key to the heart of people.
author: Ahmed Deedat

text: And now I am here, as alone as I've ever been. I am seventeen years old. This is not how it's suppose to be. This is not how my life is suppose to turn out.
author: Gayle Forman, If I Stay

text: For some people, “the point of no return” begins at the very moment their souls become aware of each others’ existence.
author: C. JoyBell C.

text: I’m not good at talking. Can’t I just nod my way through a conversation? It’s better than nodding off.
author: Jarod Kintz, At even one penny, this book would be overpriced. In fact, free is too expensive, because you'd still waste time by reading it.

text: Never judge someone by their relatives.
author: Charles Martin, Chasing Fireflies: A Novel of Discovery

text: There is something incredibly nostalgic and significant about the annual cascade of autumn leaves.
author: Joe L. Wheeler

text: It is beautiful, it is endless, it is full and yet seems empty. It hurts us.
author: Jackson Pearce, Fathomless

text: I haven’t been very impressed lately.By people,or places,or the way someone said he loved me and then slowly changed his mind.
author: Charlotte Eriksson

text: Fear can’t be reasoned with. Neither can hate. They’re like love. They’re almost identical emotions. That’s why Ares and Aphrodite like each other. Their twin sons – Fear and Panic – were spawned from both war and love.
author: Rick Riordan, The Blood of Olympus

text: You have to rely on whatever sparks you have inside.
author: Lisa Kleypas, Sugar Daddy

text: As we express our gratitude, we must never forget that the highest appreciation is not to utter words, but to live by them.
author: John F. Kennedy

text: The conflict between the will to deny horrible events and the will to proclaim them aloud is the central dialectic of psychological trauma.
author: Judith Lewis Herman, Trauma and Recovery

text: One of the deepest longings of the human soul is to be seen.
author: John O'Donohue

text: Gabe?"The newchild stirred slightly in his sleep. Jonas looked over at him. "There could be love", Jonas whispered.
author: Lois Lowry, The Giver

text: Open your eyes, train your ears, use your head. If a mind you have, then use it while you can.
author: Haruki Murakami, Hard-Boiled Wonderland and the End of the World

text: If life is a journey then let my soul travel and share your pain.
author: Santosh Kalwar

text: Life is something that happens when you can't get to sleep.
author: Fran Lebowitz

text: We carry about us the burden of what thousands of people have said and the memories of all our misfortunes. To abandon all that is to be alone, and the mind that is alone is not only innocent but young -- not in time or age, but young, innocent, alive at whatever age -- and only such a mind can see that which is truth and that which is not measurable by words.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: Confidence is like a dragon where, for every head cut off, two more heads grow back.
author: Criss Jami, Venus in Arms

text: Sentiment without action is the ruin of the soul.
author: Edward Abbey

text: The object of a New Year is not that we should have a new year. It is that we should have a new soul and a new nose; new feet, a new backbone, new ears, and new eyes. Unless a particular man made New Year resolutions, he would make no resolutions. Unless a man starts afresh about things, he will certainly do nothing effective.
author: G.K. Chesterton, A Chesterton calendar

text: Losing you is most difficult for me, but the nature of my love for you is what matters. If it distorts into half-truth, then perhaps it is better not to love you. I must keep my mind but loose you.
author: Haruki Murakami, Hard-Boiled Wonderland and the End of the World

text: The only tyrant I accept in this world is the 'still small voice' within me. And even though I have to face the prospect of being a minority of one, I humbly believe I have the courage to be in such a hopeless minority.
author: Mahatma Gandhi, The Essential Gandhi: An Anthology of His Writings on His Life, Work, and Ideas

text: Hope and Memory have one daughter and her name is Art, and she has built her dwelling far from the desperate field where men hang out their garments upon forked boughs to be banners of battle. O beloved daughter of Hope and Memory, be with me for a while.
author: W.B. Yeats

text: ‎No people whose word for 'yesterday' is the same as their word for 'tomorrow' can be said to have a firm grip on the time.
author: Salman Rushdie, Midnight's Children

text: Chronic anxiety is a state more undesirable than any other, and we will try almost any maneuver to eliminate it. Modern man is living in anxious anticipation of destruction. Such anxiety can be easily eliminated by self-destruction. As a German saying puts it: 'Better an end with terror than a terror without end.
author: Robert E. Neale, The Art of Dying

text: There are two infinities that confuse me: the one in my soul devours me; the one around me will crush me
author: Gustave Flaubert

text: Being faithful in the smallest things is the way to gain, maintain, and demonstrate the strength needed to accomplish something great.
author: Alex Harris

text: Perfection of character is this: to live each day as if it were your last, without frenzy, without apathy, without pretence.
author: Marcus Aurelius, Meditations

text: I need to figure out the secret. I need to work out how to keep things flying back to me instead of always flying away.
author: Carol Rifka Brunt, Tell the Wolves I'm Home

text: We must do our work for its own sake, not for fortune or attention or applause.
author: Steven Pressfield, The War of Art: Break Through the Blocks & Win Your Inner Creative Battles

text: Insecurities have the ability to shape and mold our minds to live with everything that’s bad; like crying on the inside, while smiling on the outside…thus creating pain…but, alas, I have the answer; forget about what you thought and enjoy (embrace) what you feel
author: Jeremy Aldana

text: A strong man cannot help a weaker unless the weaker is willing to be helped, and even then the weak man must become strong of himself; he must, by his own efforts, develop the strength which he admires in another. None but himself can alter his condition.
author: James Allen, As a Man Thinketh

text: Before you find your soul mate, you must first discover your soul.
author: Charles F. Glassman, Brain Drain The Breakthrough That Will Change Your Life

text: After every storm, there is a rainbow. If you have eyes, you will find it. If you have wisdom, you will create it. If you have love for yourself and others, you won’t need it.
author: Shannon L. Alder

text: The more I understand the mind and the human experience, the more I begin to suspect there is no such thing as unhappiness; there is only ungratefulness.
author: Steve Maraboli, Unapologetically You: Reflections on Life and the Human Experience

text: What makes a hero? Courage, strength, morality, withstanding adversity? Are these the traits that truly show and create a hero? Is the light truly the source of darkness or vice versa? Is the soul a source of hope or despair? Who are these so called heroes and where do they come from? Are their origins in obscurity or in plain sight?
author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky, Notes from Underground

text: A single day spent doing things which fail to nourish the soul is a day stolen, mutilated, and discarded in the gutter of destiny.
author: Michel Faber, The Crimson Petal and the White

text: Crying relieves pressure on soul.
author: Toba Beta, Master of Stupidity

text: I want to be the best version of myself for anyone who is going to someday walk into my life and need someone to love them beyond reason.
author: Jennifer Elisabeth, Born Ready: Unleash Your Inner Dream Girl

text: I had a dream about you last night.. you were holding a pine cone and introducing him as Gerald.
author: Nicole McKay, I Had a Dream About You

text: Insanity is often the logic of an accurate mind overtasked
author: Oliver Wendell Holmes Sr., Autocrat of the Breakfast Table

text: It’s a lack of clarity that creates chaos and frustration. Those emotions are poison to any living goal.
author: Steve Maraboli, Life, the Truth, and Being Free

text: Deep in my heart I'm concealing things that I'm longing to say. Scared to confess what I'm feeling - frightened you'll slip away.
author: Madonna

text: Life is not over because you have diabetes. Make the most of what you have, be grateful.
